question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the support his Department provides adults with ADHD to assist them to (i) get employment; and (ii) stay in employment.
answer_text: The Department for Communities is fully committed to supporting all people with disabilities and health conditions, including those with ADHD, progress towards, move into and stay in employment. Work Coaches operate in each of the Department’s 35 Jobs & Benefits Offices with a key purpose of working with individuals to support them into work where they have a health condition or disability. Each office has developed relationships with local providers, including those supporting individuals with conditions such as ADHD. The Department’s Work Psychologists team work across each of the 35 Jobs & Benefits Offices to develop the capability and confidence of Work Coaches to support people with health and disability related support needs, including those diagnosed with ADHD. This has included the delivery of 14 training sessions covering the topic of neurodiversity between 31 March 2023 and 1 April 2024.There are also a number of existing provisions delivering interventions to support people with disabilities and health conditions attain and sustain employment, including JobStart, Labour Market Partnerships, Condition Management Programme, Workable (NI), Access to Work (NI), Work Experience and Adviser Discretion Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on discussions between his Department, DFI Rivers and the local council in relation to the proposed backwater drainage scheme to address water accumulation at Lower Strabane Road, Castlederg.
answer_text: Following discussions with DfI Roads, Rivers and NI Water it has been established that the backwater drainage scheme at Lower Strabane Road, Castlederg involves the construction of a new pumping station by NI Water.As this issue is an operational matter for NI Water I have sought the requested information from NI Water, but it has not yet been provided.  I will provide you with a full response once NI Water provides the requested information. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.com   Making direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the waiting times for an ADHD diagnosis for adults in each of the last three years.
answer_text: There are currently no regionally commissioned services for adult ADHD and therefore no requirement for HSC Trusts to manage a dedicated and comparable ADHD waiting list. The information is therefore not available.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of adults diagnosed with ADHD in each of the last three years, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: My Department does not routinely collect information in regards to the ADHD diagnoses and there is no regional register or consistent recording of ADHD across the Health and Social Care (HSC) Trusts. Therefore, the number of people diagnosed with ADHD in the last three years cannot be provided.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department will introduce ADHD and autism training and awareness in schools for staff.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) and Middletown Centre for Autism (MCA) currently offer a range of training to all teachers and the educational workforce in relation to Autism.The EA has advised that Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) awareness training is not currently part of their core training offer; however, a school can make a request for specific training on supporting pupils with ADHD, which will be facilitated.  As part of the End to End (E2E) Review of Special Educational Needs (SEN), the department is examining Teacher Professional Learning (TPL) for SEN that will include initial teacher education and continuous professional learning.  Early actions will set out opportunities to develop a highly skilled professional, confident and capable workforce who are responsive to the needs of children and young people as they present in the classroom. Following receipt of recommendations from the E2E Review, I will take action to ensure high quality SEN training, including autism training, is available to all teachers and the educational workforce to enable them to be confident and capable to meet the needs of all children and young people in their classrooms.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail how the proposals contained in the Pensions (Extension of Automatic Enrolment) Bill will be funded in the long-term.
answer_text: The Pensions (Extension of Automatic Enrolment) Bill itself will not impose any costs on employers or employees at this time. The first year of the full implementation of the policy would generate costs to businesses as set out in the Regulatory Impact Assessment.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the cost implications for the proposals contained in The Smoke, Heat and Carbon Monoxide Alarms for Private Tenancies Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2024 for (i) tenants; (ii) landlords; and (iii) public bodies.
answer_text: In respect of cost implications arising from ‘The Smoke, Heat, and Carbon Monoxide Alarms for Private Tenancies Regulations (NI) 2024:		a tenant is not required to pay or contribute towards the costs of purchasing or installing the required alarms as the duty within the Regulation is imposed upon the landlord;	the cost on a landlord will be circa £200 for an average property for the deployment of the necessary long-life battery alarms with an estimated lifespan of up to 10 years.  If the landlord needs/chooses to follow the hardwired alarm route the cost per alarm is roughly the same, it is recognised extra cost will be incurred for installation or any applicable building control fee; and	in respect of cost implications for public bodies, the Landlord Registrar is unable to advise how many registered private landlords are public bodies.  This is because the legislation which introduced the Landlord Registration scheme requires a personal contact name and address to be entered rather than business/company details.  I can however advise that the regulations will have no cost for the Housing Executive, as it is a social housing provider.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he has undertaken an assessment of the impact the Prime Minister’s proposed policy for a national service scheme will have on the local economy.
answer_text: No.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the proportion of schools that conducted a Cycling Proficiency Scheme for pupils in each of the last five years; and (ii) what his Department is doing to increase this proportion.
answer_text: All primary and special schools across the North are invited to participate in the Cycling Proficiency Scheme each year. The proportion of schools that delivered the scheme over the past five years was as follows:YearNo. of SchoolsProportion (840 schools)*201934841%202010 (COVID-19)1%202125230%202231437%202315018%*Based on primary schools (x774), special schools (x40), independent schools (x14), preps (x12)There was a drop in numbers in 2023/24 to 150 schools, which was due to a reduction in funding available for the scheme.  I confirmed the reinstatement of this funding for the current year and I’m pleased to say that to date almost 300 schools have indicated their intention to participate.  The annual letter to launch the scheme was issued to schools and registered instructors and, to further promote the scheme this year, a promotional video was also produced and posted on my Department’s social media.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on the six Sustainable Utilisation of Livestock Slurry schemes funded by his Department.
answer_text: To help address the issue of excess nutrients from livestock slurry, my Department delivered a Sustainable Utilisation of Livestock Slurry (SULS) Small Business Research Initiative (SBRI) Phase 1 competition in 2023. £600,000 was awarded to six companies to develop proposals to address the core aim of nutrient separation / processing of livestock slurry to reduce surplus phosphorus within NI agriculture and ensure efficient recycling of organic nutrients.In September and October 2023, the six companies delivered their final reports. Solutions centred around using separated livestock slurry as a feedstock for anaerobic digestion plants to produce biomethane.	Following the success of Phase 1, my officials have submitted a business case to the Department of Finance for a follow up project – SULS Phase 2. Subject to approval, the next phase will focus on turning the proof of concepts already proposed in Phase 1 into reality, with at scale separation of livestock slurry planned for late 2024.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why bus and rail fares were not subject to a price freeze this year.
answer_text: The funding of an effective public transport service is a key priority for my Department. However, my Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. Translink have been operating in a challenging situation with an increasing cost base, and consequently an increase in fares has been required to ensure the ongoing financial viability of the corporation , and future delivery of services. Translink will however continue to offer a range of good value fares and I would encourage users of public transport to look for the best value options available.   In the meantime, the value of our public transport services cannot be overstated, and I will continue to work with Executive colleagues to ensure appropriate investment is made.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the actions his Department is taking to improve epilepsy services in the Western Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: My department is taking forward a Regional Review of Neurology Services to improve services for patients with neurological conditions including epilepsy across the region, including the Western Health & Social Care Trust.The Review, is tasked with identifying the optimum configuration of neurology services for the next 10-15 years, determining the associated investment required and outlining an implementation plan. As part of the Review, workstreams were established to consider key parts of the Neurology pathway including Outpatient referral and long-term support. The Neurology Review Team is now in the final stages of developing priorities for change which, it is anticipated, will drive improvements across Neurology services, including access to Outpatient assessment and diagnosis.  It is anticipated that the Final Report of the Neurology Review will be completed in the coming months.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the three projects approved so far through the Social Capital Loan Scheme.
answer_text: The loans so far (as of 30 May 2024) approved by Community Finance Ireland through the Social Capital Loan Scheme are:To Crusaders FC for a 3G pitch and floodlightingTo Draperstown Celtic FC for a 3G pitch and floodlightingTo Kilmore Rec FC for a 3G pitch and floodlightingTo North Belfast Enterprise for property refurbishment

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) what extra data is necessary to inform long-term childcare policy development; and (ii) for his assessment of whether the £2.5 million allocated for this work is sufficient.
answer_text: Comprehensive and authoritative data is required on a range of issues including the financial position of providers, fees charged, staff numbers, qualifications, rates of pay, training needs and the capacity to support children with Special Education Needs and Disabilities.  This is needed to inform the development of a longer-term model which balances affordability for parents and sustainability for providers.  Statistically representative data on parental views, attitudes and motivations is also required to better understand the likely impacts of various interventions, including on labour market participation decisions.  The £2.5m earmarked for this work in 2024/25 will enable the collection of baseline data.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his Department's plans in relation to a smoking ban following reports that the UK legislation is not progressing.
answer_text: I am incredibly disappointed that the UK Tobacco and Vapes Bill was unable to be captured in the “wash-up” process before parliament was prorogued. This is especially the case as it came so quickly after the Assembly agreed to my predecessor’s legislative consent motion. Future decisions regarding such legislation and the territorial extent will fall to the government which forms following the election.  However, given the cross-party support for the Bill, I remain optimistic that these proposals will likely be picked up again quickly.  There are advantages to the smokefree generation measures being taken forward on a UK wide basis, however we will review our position once the incoming UK Government intentions have been clarified.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the provision of Irish-medium education in West Tyrone.
answer_text: The statutory responsibility for securing sufficient schools in an area rests with the Education Authority (EA) and, in the case of Irish-medium (IM) education, the Department funds Comhairle na Gaelscolaíochta (CnaG) to identify where demand for IM education exists, and to work with parents, and local schools and the respective educational sector with a view to developing that demand.CnaG was established by the Department in August 2000 and its remit is to encourage and facilitate the strategic development of, and provides guidance and advice to, the Irish medium education sector.Within the West Tyrone area there are two IM primary schools:-Gaelscoil Uí Dhochartaigh and Gaelscoil na gCrann (both schools also provide 26 full-time statutory nursery places). For the 2023/24 year the two schools have a combined approved enrolment of 355 places with an actual combined enrolment of 280 pupils and there are 86 available places. The schools admitted 38 pupils into Year One for the 58 approved admission places.At the conclusion of the process for Primary Admission to Year One, the two schools have received 51 First Preference applications for the 58 approved places for the 2024/25 year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his plans for active travel in West Tyrone for the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: My Department is currently developing an Active Travel Network Delivery Plan for the North, including a full update of the design guidance and proposed active travel network plans for larger towns and cities. We plan to consult on draft proposals for the Active Travel Network Plan later in 2024.Having decided on indicative budget allocations, my officials can now consider the detailed programmes of work across the Department’s areas of responsibility, including active travel schemes within the West Tyrone constituency.Officials will then list all planned active travel schemes within my Department’s 2024/25 annual report to local councils.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what steps her Department is taking to promote tourism in the West Tyrone constituency.
answer_text: Tourism NI (TNI) has regularly promoted the West Tyrone area through its seasonal marketing campaigns which are targeted to specific market segments based on extensive research. The campaigns run in both the north and south of Ireland. One of the most recent pieces of promotional activity was the launch of a major Spring advertising campaign on 22 January 2024 which ran until 31 March.  The campaign featured TV, radio, press, press supplements, outdoor, social, and digital advertising, all underpinned by a comprehensive PR strategy. Full page press advertorials dedicated to the Fermanagh and Tyrone areas including West Tyrone were featured in National press titles both North and South.A double page spread dedicated to the Fermanagh and Tyrone areas, including West Tyrone, was contained within a 24-page TNI consumer supplement that was inserted into several key National press titles both North and South and featured amongst others, the Ulster American Folk Park and An Creagan.Planning is in train for the next campaign due to take place in the autumn, and with regional balance one of TNI’s key priorities, West Tyrone will again be featured.TNI also works with its partner Tourism Ireland to host international media, with West Tyrone featuring regularly in many media itineraries. There were 6 group trips for International Media in 2023 where West Tyrone featured in the itinerary, as well as individual visits from journalists to the area.One example of this was the 2024 British Daily Telegraph Embrace a Giant Spirit campaign featuring Adam Parkinson’s ‘Roam with Adam’ which included the Sperrins Giant Trail and Gortin Forest Park.RTE 1 Television, Tracks and Trails, featured the Sperrins as part of a TNI commissioned programme by the TNI Dublin office which aired in April exploring the Sperrin Mountains and the Gortin Glen Forest Park in County Tyrone. A media trip in February 2024 included some attractions within the area including the Ulster American Folk Park, Glenpark Estate and An Creagan. West Tyrone is promoted on www.discovernorthernireland.com as part of the wider County, with suggestions on what to see and do, where to stay and places to eat. Recent examples have included The Sperrin Sculpture Trail, Todd’s Leap Activity Centre, the Ulster American Folk Park, and The Ritual Landscapes of West Tyrone. Themed content pages including the NI Spirits Trail, Family Activities & Attractions and Best Cheap and Free Things to Do all feature products from the West Tyrone area.West Tyrone regularly features across the Discover Northern Ireland social media pages. For example, the Ulster American Folk Park has been highlighted in several posts over the Christmas 2023 period as well as more recently in the promotion the Omagh Bluegrass Festival. The Sperrin Sculpture trail has also been featured across both Instagram and Facebook as a ‘bucket list’ activity for 2024. TNI also works closely with the tourism industry in West Tyrone and across the North

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what action he is taking to resolve the backlog in occupational therapy assessments for elderly patients in West Tyrone.
answer_text: My officials have consulted with the Public Health Agency and are aware of the significant workforce challenges and increasing demand that the Western Health & Social Care Trust is experiencing and the impact that this is having on the Occupational Therapy service.  My Department has taken proactive measures to increase the Occupational Therapy workforce of the future, commissioning an additional 28 undergraduate places starting in September 2025, bringing the total annual intake onto Ulster University’s Occupational Therapy undergraduate programme to 93 each year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how his Department is supporting libraries in West Tyrone
answer_text: My department continues to support the network of five public libraries in the West Tyrone constituency, providing Libraries NI with the resources to meet the day-to-day operational needs of the service and through investment in infrastructure and facilities. Over the past five years capital investment totalling some £534,000 has been provided to support specific facility improvement projects delivered by Libraries NI as noted in the table below:Capital Project Details:   LNI Capital Projects in West Tyrone Constituency 2019 to 2024 Strabane Library - changing place facility £0 £0 £63,000 £0 £0 Omagh Library - roof upgrade £238,000 £0 £0 £0 £0 Omagh Library - out of hours library £138,000 Omagh Library - toilet upgrade £45,000 £0 £0 £0 £0 Omagh Library - automatic vehicle barrier £0 £0 £6,000 £0 £0 Castlederg Library - LED lighting £0 £0 £0 £19,000 £0 Castlederg Library - roof upgrade £0 £0 £0 £0 £25,000 Total £421,000 £0 £69,000 £19,000 £25,000

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) the number of sightings of Asian hornets reported to his Department in the last 12 months, broken down by constituency; and (ii) any actions being taken to support the local bee population.
answer_text: (i) To date, there have been no confirmed reports of Asian hornets anywhere in Northern Ireland. (ii) DAERA have in place a rapid response contingency plan for Asian hornets, with a focus on hornet eradication and protecting bees. DAERA has been actively engaged with the development and implementation of actions in the 2021-2025 All-Ireland Pollinator Plan, to help protect all pollinating insects. The 186 actions focus on sustainably managing private, public and farmland for pollinators, particularly bees. Actions span across four themes; providing food, safety and shelter for pollinators and also improving our knowledge of populations through research. Actions are being delivered through various means, including working closely with grant-funded partners, such as Butterfly Conservation and Buglife, to raise awareness of the Plan, and develop pollinator actions into project work plans.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he plans to review and update the Tackling Rural Poverty and Social Isolation Framework.
answer_text: My Department has arranged for a review of its Tackling Rural Poverty and Social Isolation (TRPSI) Framework to be undertaken which will identify how effectively it has been implemented and help inform DAERA’s future policy in relation to addressing poverty and social isolation in rural areas. The review has now commenced and is due to report back in early 2025. 		To ensure that the review is properly informed the review will include engagement with a wide range of stakeholders including public authorities and rural community organisations.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will consider issues in relation to the burden of proof when patient records have been lost when responding to the Infected Blood Inquiry Report.
answer_text: I welcome the publication of the final report from the Infected Blood Inquiry and am grateful for Sir Brian Langstaff and his team’s thorough work in producing this comprehensive report, which was published in seven volumes, comprising over 2,500 pages and 12 recommendations, including over 50 individual actions. I am acutely mindful and aware of the life-changing impacts and financial hardship that people have endured as a result of receiving a devastating diagnosis following treatment with NHS contaminated blood. The health service’s use of contaminated blood in the 1970s and 1980s resulted in untold suffering and loss and on the day the final report was published, my predecessor, Minister Swann again reiterated my Department’s apology to all those in the infected and/or affected community in Northern Ireland. They were failed by the system that should have been there to help them.The detailed recommendations of the IBI Final Report are wide-ranging, cutting across a range of areas in my Department, including Quality, Safety and Improvement and Workforce Policy, as well as the culture and working practices of the wider Executive, Civil Service and the Health and Social Care system.  Addressing these will require a collective effort and coordination of actions and communications, in collaboration with the infected and affected community.I want to carefully consider the details contained in the report and its recommendations and therefore I am not yet in a position to respond in detail now. I am, however, committed to working with my ministerial colleagues on a four nations basis, as well as across NI Executive, in taking action to respond. I have no doubt that the work of the Inquiry and its recommendations will help to inform and shape the delivery of healthcare in Northern Ireland in the future.On Tuesday 21st May in Parliament, Paymaster General and Minister for the Cabinet Office, John Glen MP, made an announcement and gave details of the compensation scheme that is being set up, following the recommendations by Sir Brian Langstaff KC in his second interim report.  The UK Governments amendments to the Victims and Prisoners Bill have ensured that victims in Northern Ireland will be eligible to apply to the UK-wide compensation scheme, which the Minister for the Cabinet Office has said will be set up before the end of this year.  Whilst the UK Government is in the lead on compensation, I understand that there will be a process of engagement with the infected blood community in the coming weeks as the detail of the Scheme is finalised and this will include discussion on issues such as lost or missing medical records. The UKG intention is that the compensations scheme will as user-friendly, supportive and stress-free as possible, while being consistent with the government’s approach to protect against fraud.  The compensation scheme is being designed in line with advice from the Expert Group, to minimise the burden on those applying. P

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) what discussions have taken place with his Executive colleagues on the Northern Ireland Food Strategy Framework; and (ii) when the framework will be published.
answer_text: (i) Food cuts across the responsibilities of a number of Ministers, including for Economy, Communities, Health, Education, Justice and Finance, as well as my own responsibilities in relation to agriculture, environment and rural affairs.I will be writing to my Executive colleagues shortly seeking their comments on a draft Northern Ireland Food Strategy Framework, subject to these I hope to table a paper at a future Executive meeting and seek agreement to publish the Framework.  ii) Ultimately, the timing for publication will be decided at that meeting.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the indicative spend of his Department's Blue/Green Fund for the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: The Blue Green Infrastructure Fund was launched in 2020 and ended in 2023.  No budget has been allocated in the current financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the actions his Department will commit to in relation to rural development in West Tyrone.
answer_text: Following the agreement of the 2024-25 Budget by the Executive, I am carefully considering funding allocations across my Department. As part of that I am committed to prioritising essential front-line services and addressing regional imbalance, and I intend to launch an equality impact consultation which will help inform my final opening budget decisions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he plans to increase the Rate Support Grant.
answer_text: The funding available for my Department is set within the context of a constrained financial position for 2024/25, and the overall Departmental budget position will be considered against competing priorities. I will consider the funding for councils, including Rates Support Grant, in the context of my Department's overall budget priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he intends to undertake a review of the Taxis Act (Northern Ireland) 2008.
answer_text: I currently have no plans to complete a review of the Taxi Act (NI) 2008. I’m sure you will appreciate that  I have many competing priorities within my Department and a review of the Taxis Act would involve significant resources. I can assure you that my Department is committed to working with and helping the taxi industry address the range of issues that it faces.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) to detail the number of electric vehicle charge points; and (ii) how many of these are out of order.
answer_text: The provision and operation of publicly accessible electric vehicle (EV) charge points is a commercially led operation. Responsibility for the maintenance and installation of those charge points rests solely with the charge point operators (CPO’s). In this commercially led market, my officials continue to engage with EV owners and provide advice to CPO’s on operational matters. Latest statistics show there are 577 publicly available EV charge points in the North. This represents a growth of 48% in the total number of available EV charge points since April 2023. Relevant charge point operators are best placed to advise of any maintenance issues and plans for the installation of further charge points.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the planned improvements to the sewerage network in West Tyrone.
answer_text: Planned improvements to the sewerage network is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and have been advised as follows:NI Water’s systems categorise investments by council area and do not differentiate by constituency. However, NI Water has endeavoured to identify the proposed wastewater investments for PC21 that are specific to West Tyrone.PC21 Direct Investment in West TyroneProject Name PC21 Investment (£m)Strabane Drainage Area Unsatisfactory Intermittent Discharge - Park Road Storm Overflow2.2Ballycolman Stormwater Separation 1.7Sion Mills Stormwater Separation1.6Mountfield Wastewater Treatment Works5.0Lough Macrory Wastewater Treatment Works4.8All future investments are subject to statutory approval being in place, land acquisition (where appropriate) and the availability of funding.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.comMaking direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will extend the Social Work Student Incentive Scheme to include those studying outside Northern Ireland, having been unable to secure a place here.
answer_text: The Social Work Student Incentive Scheme is available for eligible students, domicile in Northern Ireland, who study to achieve a NISCC approved Degree in Social Work at undergraduate level here. The rationale for restricting the Scheme to NI is based on evidence which found that more than 70% of students funded to study courses outside of Northern Ireland did not return to work here, whereas, the majority of local graduates remain and join the workforce in NI.Unfortunately, this means that individuals who study social work outside of NI place themselves outside the remit of the Scheme.Regrettably, given the extremely challenging budgetary situation, the Department is unable to provide any more assistance to students on professional training programmes than it does at present.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on discussions between his Department and the Western Health and Social Care Trust to find a long-term solution for GP services in Fintona.
answer_text: DoH/SPPG  recognise that being able to access GP practices when we need to do so is important for all of us.  However, GP practices are under significant pressures at present as they deal with immediate demands and the consequences of wider service pressures.  DoH/SPPG continue to work with all GP Practices across Northern Ireland to maintain the provision of GMS services to the resident population.With regards to plans going forward for GP Practices where GMS contracts have been handed back - including Fintona Practice - DoH/SPPG are currently working closely with GP colleagues, GP Federations and Health & Social Care Trust colleagues in order to identify and take forward long-term solutions for these GP Practices.In the meantime, the current Western Trust management arrangement should provide stability for the Practice and patients should continue to contact the practice as normal.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on discussions between his Department and the Western Health and Social Care Trust to find a long-term solution for GP services in Dromore and Trillick.
answer_text: DoH/SPPG  recognise that being able to access GP practices when we need to do so is important for all of us.  However, GP practices are under significant pressures at present as they deal with immediate demands and the consequences of wider service pressures.  DoH/SPPG continue to work with all GP Practices across Northern Ireland to maintain the provision of GMS services to the resident population.With regards to plans going forward for GP Practices where GMS contracts have been handed back - including Dromore and Trillick Practice - DoH/SPPG are currently working closely with GP colleagues, GP Federations and Health & Social Care Trust colleagues in order to identify and take forward long-term solutions for these GP Practices.In the meantime, the current Western Trust management arrangement should provide stability for the Practice and patients should continue to contact the practice as normal.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether she will review the cap on the number of students studying social work at university, given the staff shortages in the sector.
answer_text: The number of places commissioned for student social workers each year is determined by the funding available to the Department of Health.  In recent years, 260 social work training places have been commissioned at Queens University Belfast and Ulster University Magee. In the past four years my Department has worked with the Open University (OU) and other stakeholders to develop an approved OU Degree in Social Work in NI. The Department now commissions 25 additional social work training places via the OU.I am pleased to confirm that I have recently secured a commitment to commission a further 40 social work training places commencing 2024/5, which will, in time, help to alleviate some of the workforce pressures in the sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 10786/22-27, why his Department has not given these staff a promotion on a permanent basis.
answer_text: Staff in temporary promotions cannot be made permanent automatically. NICS recruitment policy requires that substantive filling of posts is done through NICS HR led recruitment competitions. All NI Civil Servants must be appointed on merit from these fair and open competitions. My officials are committed to filling these posts substantively as soon as available competitions and supply permit. They continue to engage with NICS HR to progress recruitment competitions that will fill more posts substantively and speed up recruitment processes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what further actions his Department will take to advance shared education.
answer_text: My Department is continuing to implement the Mainstreaming Shared Education Strategy (2022) via associated phased delivery plans, with the aim of establishing Shared Education as a core feature of educational provision in Northern Ireland, that is affordable, sustainable and accessible to all. Alongside continued support for mainstreamed partnerships (subject to availability of resources), the new PEACE PLUS ‘Shared Learning Together’ programme provides a critical vehicle for the further advancement of Shared Education over the coming years across schools, pre-school and youth settings. My officials will continue to engage closely with colleagues in the Special European Union Programmes Body and Republic of Ireland to ensure successful delivery of related projects. The Ballycastle Shared Education Campus commenced construction on 6 May 2024.  The Strule and other Shared Education Campus capital projects will continue to be progressed in line with confirmation of access to funding and approved business cases.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what action he is taking to increase the diversity of Non-executive Board Members appointed by his Department.
answer_text: My Department is fully committed to ensuring that Non-Executive Board Members (NEBMs) on its Management Board and the boards of its public bodies are as fully reflective of society as possible whilst ensuring that appointees are selected on the basis of merit through fair, open and transparent processes. ALB Board appointments are regulated by the Commissioner for Public Appointments NI (CPANI). The CPANI Code of Practice contains a range of principles and practices to be followed, including diversity considerations. My Department undertakes various activities to encourage a diverse range of applications including social media usage for greater outreach, and engagement with organisations that champion under-represented groups to attract applications. DfC NEBM appointments also follow the rigour applied within public appointment processes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he plans to bring our legislation in line with the Equality Act 2010, as suggested by the Equality Commission.
answer_text: The Equality Act 2010 replaced previous anti-discrimination laws in Great Britain with a single Act. Policy and legislative responsibility for the areas covered in the Act is the responsibility of more than one NI Executive department.I am currently considering the next steps to be taken in the development of Executive Social Inclusion Strategies. This will include consideration of the need for a review and reform of equality legislation for the areas which my Department is responsible for.Final decisions on the content and timings of the implementation of the Strategies will be subject to Executive Agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the implementation of the Traveller Child in Education Action Framework.
answer_text: The Department’s Traveller Child in Education Action Framework was published in 2013, in response to a 2011 report by the Taskforce on Traveller Education, which had been established to assist the Department to develop an action plan on Traveller education.  The Department is now commencing a review of the Framework.  The aim of this review is to:consider the effectiveness of the current framework and associated educational services for Traveller children; explore existing funding arrangements for Travellers in education; anddevelop and agree a revised forward-looking policy framework, which delivers the best outcomes for Traveller children and young people and contributes to achieving their full education potential.The review of the Framework will:consider how well it has achieved its stated aims and objectives;consider to what extent targets within the current framework have been delivered upon and explore options for improvement;identify any remaining actions / gaps against targets; identify emerging or new needs that need to be addressed within a revised Action Framework; andconsider additional funding factors provided to schools to support Traveller education.Stakeholder engagement will be at the forefront of this work.  A Stakeholder Engagement group has recently been established to provide advice on stakeholder engagement, evidence gathering, and to help develop potential evidence informed interventions. The group includes members of the Traveller community with lived experience, Traveller support organisations and other statutory and voluntary groups with an interest in Traveller education.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of a report by the Trussell Trust which states that there are record levels of need for food banks in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Trussell Trusts latest statistics indicate their network has seen an 11% increase in people seeking assistance in 2023/24 and this would reflect feedback my Department receives through engagement with organisations and people in local communities who have seen an increase in demand for support as a result of the increased cost of living.My Department does not directly fund foodbanks however we have been working with local Councils on a co-design basis to roll out Social Supermarket models across Northern Ireland as an alternative to foodbanks. The aim of Social Supermarkets is to offer a longer term, more sustainable, response to food poverty by providing wraparound services that seek to address the underlying reasons why someone requires assistance alongside access to food.  In the broader context, I remain committed to delivering long-term, sustainable solutions to poverty, including food poverty, in all its forms for our communities right across Northern Ireland. I am currently considering the next steps to be taken in relation to the development of an Anti-Poverty Strategy, which will be subject to Executive agreement.	In the short term details of government support for those struggling with the Cost of Living are available at: https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/campaigns/cost-living

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what support his Department is providing to increase the safety of Translink staff.
answer_text: The safety of our public transport workers and passengers is vitally important. Our public transport workers deserve to feel safe doing their job and should not be subjected to attacks, either verbal or physical.Translink holds a zero-tolerance policy towards violence and aggression and it condemns all instances of physical assault and verbal abuse on colleagues and passengers. In response to previous attacks, Translink put in place a series of measures to ensure the safety of staff and customers including. Temporary curtailment or alteration to some bus services;Translink Operational Personnel working within the PSNI Command Centre;Additional Translink mobile support vehicles being deployed supporting drivers and services where required;Regular update meetings between Translink and the Trade Unions;Daily reviews with PSNI; andEngagement with local communities.Translink provide training and equipment to assist employees undertake their job safely and to ensure the safety of customers. Risk assessments are also carried out and continually reviewed. Controls include personal issue body cams, conflict resolution training, radios, and assault protection screens. “No excuse for abuse” posters are displayed in areas that show issues.  Translink continues to engage on a regular basis with the PSNI at a senior level to ensure the continued safety for all their customers, staff and passenger services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what action his Department is taking to address the 11 per cent increase in food bank usage in Northern Ireland in the last year.
answer_text: My Department has been working with local Councils on a co-design basis to roll out Social Supermarket models across Northern Ireland as an alternative to foodbanks. The aim of Social Supermarkets is to offer a longer term, more sustainable, response to food insecurity by providing wraparound services in addition to access to food to address the underlying problems contributing to household food insecurity.  My Department’s Make the Call Wraparound Service aims to ensure that all individuals and their families are receiving the benefits, supports and services they are entitled to claim. The Service has a team of Community Outreach Officers, who conduct home visits with vulnerable people through age, disability, illness, or adverse circumstances, to provide face to face assistance and support. The team works in partnership with a wide variety of organisations across government, and voluntary and community sectors including a number of Social Supermarkets to provide practical benefit support.My Department’s Discretionary Support service is unique to Northern Ireland and provides a fast and responsive means of delivering short term financial support.Discretionary Support may be able to offer help towards short-term living expenses or household items via either non-repayable grants or an interest free loan to people experiencing financial difficulties, provided a person meets the eligibility criteria. Anyone whose annual income is below the annual income threshold of £26,769.60 is eligible to apply regardless of whether they are in work or in receipt of benefits.  More information about the financial support available through the Discretionary Support scheme can be found on the NIDirect website www.nidirect.gov.uk/campaigns/finance-support.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the Strabane Public Realm Scheme.
answer_text: I am currently considering this project for approval alongside a number of other capital priorities across my Department.  The member will of course understand the wider context, in terms of a difficult capital budget settlement set against a number of competing priorities for my Department.  If I decide to approve the project, it will then require approval from the Department of Finance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice what action she is taking to increase the diversity of Non-executive Board Members appointed by her Department.
answer_text: Non-Executive members of the Department’s Management Board are not Ministerial appointments.   All opportunities within the Department, however, are advertised widely to maximise the number and diversity of applicants.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how his Department will assess the success of the intermediate-rent policy in terms of (i) value for money; (ii) rent levels; and (iii) eligibility criteria.
answer_text: Successful implementation of the Intermediate Rent policy will see the delivery of an increased supply of affordable homes for rent, a reduction in affordability issues experienced by those accessing these homes, and the availability of high quality, more secure homes with support services for Intermediate Rent tenants.A competition, launched on 15th May, will lead to a funding award to an Intermediate Rent Operator who will deliver homes and act as landlord.  Comprehensive monitoring arrangements will ensure the collection of the necessary data and information to monitor and evaluate the achievement of value for money in this expenditure, monitor that rent setting complies with policy requirements, and confirm that the Operator is adhering to the eligibility requirements detailed in the policy.  Details on the competition can be found at: https://www.communities-ni.gov.uk/publications/funding-deliver-northern-ireland-intermediate-rent-housing-scheme-application-guidance.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the legislative programme of the Executive for the remainder of the 2022-2027 mandate.

To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the number of children awaiting an autism assessment; and (ii) the waiting times for an autism assessment for children, in the Western Health and Social Care Trust in each of the last three years.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 11285/22-27, for an update on discussions between his Department and the Council for Catholic Maintained Schools in relation to increasing the admission and enrollment numbers at Dean Maguirc College, Carrickmore.
answer_text: On 9 May 2024, I met with the Council for Catholic Maintained Schools (CCMS), Department officials and representatives from Dean Maguirc College, to discuss the admissions and enrolment numbers at the school. I understand that CCMS subsequently indicated that it will bring forward a Development Proposal (DP) to seek to increase the admissions and enrolment numbers at the school, via the DP process under Article 14 of the Education and Libraries (Northern Ireland) order 1986.  CCMS will work with the school to bring this DP forward in due course. In the meantime, the school can continue to request additional places on an annual basis through the Temporary Variation process, until a DP is brought forward and decided upon.

answer_text: The Department does not hold this information.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer AQO 352/22-27.
answer_text: AQO 352/22-27 was answered on 21 May 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to list the backlog in cases for the Planning Appeals Commission for each of the last five years.
answer_text: Whilst the Northern Ireland Courts and Tribunals Service has sponsorship responsibility for the Planning Appeals Commission (PAC), the Commission is an independent appellant body. Thus, similarly to the judiciary, it is completely independent of the department in its handling of cases and decision-making.It would not be appropriate for me as Minister of Justice to comment on matters that encroach on the independence of the Chief Commissioner. I would, therefore, direct the member to the Chief Commissioner’s statement in the 22/23 Annual Review, in respect of the timeliness of decision-making.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what specific actions relating to the Planning Appeals Commission have been identified by the Planning Improvement Programme.
answer_text: Although there are no actions in the Planning Improvement Programme (PIP) relating specifically to the PAC, a number of actions, including those relating to the introduction of Validation checklists and improving the Local Development Plan process, require collaborative working with key stakeholders, including the PAC, to ensure successful outcomes.  Responsibility for the PAC sits outside my Department.  I am however committed to making necessary improvements to all parts of the planning system and intend to meet with my Ministerial colleague, Naomi Long MLA to consider how we can work together to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of the planning system.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what action he has taken to reduce the number of staff in his Department who have been temporarily promoted for over three years.
To ask the Minister for Communities when he plans to legislate to abolish the Local Government Staff Commission.
answer_text: On 15 November 2021 the former Minister for Communities sought the views of stakeholders on whether there were any reasons that the Executive decision in 2014 to dissolve the Local Government Staff Commission should be reconsidered. However, due to the collapse of the Executive, a decision on whether the Local Government Staff Commission should be dissolved was not considered by the former Minister.Any decision to dissolve the Local Government Staff Commission would need to be considered by the Executive due to its cross-cutting nature. Subordinate legislation, subject to the affirmative resolution procedure, would also be required to give effect to any potential dissolution of the Local Government Staff Commission.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on discussions to extend the Wild Atlantic Way to Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Discussions are ongoing and it is not yet possible to provide a definitive timeline.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of interventions his Department's has made in tackling (i) Japanese Knotweed; and (ii) Giant Hogweed in each of the last five years.
answer_text: My Department has tackled both of these species in a number of different ways, although our powers differ between the 2 species.	Japanese knotweed is not listed in the Invasive Alien Species (Enforcement and Permitting) Order Northern Ireland) 2019 so it is not illegal to have it on your land, nor is there a statutory mechanism to require a land owner to control it.	Following a public consultation in 2019, the Department designated Giant Hogweed as a Widely Spread Species (WSS) which now obligates all landowners to initiate management measures to try and reduce its distribution throughout Northern Ireland by 50% by 2030.	My Department tackles Invasive Non-Native Species through enforcement via the Invasive Alien Species Order, conditioning of planning applications, invasives management in our own country parks, Environmental Farming Scheme measures (capital works to Japanese knotweed and giant hogweed on semi-natural habitats), works assents on designated sites and many projects supported by the Environment and Challenge Funds, and Water Quality Improvement Grants. Treatment of invasive species is conditioned on all of these funding streams. However, DAERA does not hold species specific data on these interventions. 	Please see table below for numbers of agreements with farmers and other landowners with specific invasives prescriptions held by my Invasive Species team in NIEA:YearEFS AgreementsIAS RegulationInter Unit Planning or Works on Designated sites consultationsJKGHGHUnspecified Invasive Species20193nilnilnil202052511220216313512202268543820235876272024N/AN/A1914Totals2521335103

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the effectiveness of the heavy goods vehicle restrictions introduced by his Department for Clady village.
answer_text: Officials in my department have received further feedback from residents of Clady who have confirmed that the weight restriction is generally considered to have been effective in reducing the number of Heavy Goods Vehicles passing through the village.No further surveys of the effectiveness of the restriction are planned.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the rationale for ruling out a public inquiry into the murder of Arlene Arkinson.
answer_text: In making my decision I took time to carefully consider everything that had been put before me both verbally and in writing by the Arkinson family and by their legal representative.In weighing up the relevant factors and in coming to my conclusion that there were not sufficient grounds for me to establish a public inquiry into this tragic case, I also took account of the Coroner’s detailed inquest findings, the Police Ombudsman report into the handling of the police investigation into Arlene’s disappearance and the significant changes to police missing person policies.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) his assessment of whether the £5 million allocated towards GP indemnity will be sufficient; and (ii) a timescale for deciding a long-term model for future provision.
answer_text: The recently agreed 2024/25 General Medical Services (GMS) contract is an important step forward to achieving greater stability in General Practice.   The contract makes significant progress against key aims identified by GPs in their negotiations, including provision of dedicated funding for indemnity costs.  A key aim of the new contract arrangements is to provide GPs with greater certainty over their income throughout the year and the specific provision of £5m of funding towards indemnity costs is part of this overall basket of measures designed to support the ongoing sustainability of primary care.  In the context of a very disappointing budget settlement for my Department, I believe that this ring-fenced allocation will make an important contribution towards supporting GPs with their clinical negligence indemnity costs.In terms of the long-term model for indemnity provision, my Department remains committed to identifying solutions to address this issue for Northern Ireland GPs. Work on options appraisal is being taken forward through the business case process which will result in the identification of a proposed way forward for GP indemnity in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail what response they have had from the Secretary of State in relation to the request to extend the deadline to apply for the Troubles Permanent Disablement Payment Scheme.
answer_text: We wrote to the Secretary of State on 17 April in support of the extension of the deadline for eligible applicants to avail of backdated payments, to align with the closing date of the Scheme.We have had no response to date.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how much his Department will save by taking part in the No Mow May initiative.
answer_text: My Department’s approach to verge management was recently changed to increase the focus on environmental benefit, without compromising on roads safety or our duties to deal with noxious weeds. This change occurred because I recognise the environmental benefits of road verges and our approach seeks to allow wildflowers and wildlife to thrive. The month of May is very important as bees and butterflies are actively foraging for food, sourced from dandelions and other flowering plants, found in grassy areas. By refraining from grass-cutting during May, where possible, we are also helping to tackle pollution as well as locking away the very significant carbon below our feet.By reducing the areas of verge cut on many roads to a single swath or, where possible without compromising on roads safety, reducing to a single cut later in the season, it is possible to increase the focus on environmental benefit. This approach will deliver benefits in promoting biodiversity and is not designed to be a cost saving measure.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what level of additional funding will be required to deliver the Community Pharmacy Strategic Plan 2030.
answer_text: The Strategic Plan brings together four existing inter-related programmes of work led by the Department, to support the delivery of the six strategic priorities outlined in the Community Pharmacy Strategic Plan. Those four work programmes relate to: Service Development; Legislative Reform; Pharmacy Workforce Review; and e-Pharmacy.Whilst some aspects of these programmes are already in train and are funded, full delivery of the strategic vision outlined in the plan is subject to securing additional funding over the next six years, establishing new contractual arrangements, and taking forward further legislative changes. This means that implementation will be phased. The Department is currently starting to develop an investment plan to identify likely funding required across these programmes of work and will be drafting associated business cases to seek this additional funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) how often the single use vapes working group has met; and (ii) for his assessment of whether the target date of 1 April 2025 for a total ban on single use vapes is deliverable.
answer_text: In October 2023, the Prime Minister announced the UK Government’s intention to create a smoke free generation which included measures to tackle the alarming rise in youth vaping.  Since then, government officials working together through the four nations disposable vapes working group, have met on a monthly basis to develop policy options and discuss approaches.Whilst timelines are extremely ambitious, my Department is working at pace and is currently on schedule to meet the UK wide coming into force date of 1st April 2025.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he will be launching the public consultation on biennial MOT testing.
answer_text: I have asked my officials to identify the resource implications for launching a public consultation on biennial MOT testing.  This will involve engagement with stakeholders such as insurance companies, road safety organisations and others so that the consultation at its launch will present a comprehensive view on biennial MOT testing.  I anticipate the earliest this could take place is in the second half of this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will answer AQW 10790/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 10790 22-27 was answered on 15 May 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 10312/22-27, whether his Department has made a bid to this fund to help pay for the upgrading of the A5.
answer_text: The Union Connectivity Development Fund was set up to consider projects that could be taken to Strategic Outline Business Case and be completed by end of March 2025. The A5 is at a much more advanced stage of development to be considered through this Fund.I have however just recently written letters seeking funding from the British Government.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he plans to review the difficulty of the Driver Theory Test given (i) the 14.5 per cent drop in the pass rate since 2020-21; and (ii) that fewer drivers pass in Northern Ireland than the rest of Great Britain.
answer_text: I have no plans to review the difficulty of the theory test as it is important to ensure that new drivers have a sufficient level of relevant road safety knowledge and hazard perception skills to prepare them for driving unsupervised when they obtain their full licence.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, further to the suspension of the Curraghinalt Project (Dalradian) Public Inquiry by the Planning Appeals Commission, when his Department will be in a position to supply the information they request.
answer_text: My officials issued a direction to the Appeals Commission, on 2 May 2024, to hold a public local inquiry to consider representations received in relation to the newly submitted Abstraction & Impoundment Licences. There is currently a 28 day public consultation period ongoing. Any new representations received by my Department, during this period, will be sent to the Appeals Commission, along with any additional requested documents, immediately on conclusion of the consultation period, on 30 May 2024.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the future of St Lucia Barracks, Omagh.
answer_text: The St Lucia site is jointly owned by my Department and Ministry of Defence. The Historic Core remains in the ownership of MoD, who have been pressing DfI to accept this part of the site. On 8 December 2023 following legal advice that the matter engaged with the Northern Ireland (Executive Formation etc) Act 2022, the Permanent Secretary agreed that there was a public interest in making a decision to advise MoD that DfI would not accept the Historic Core. Officials will now  engage with MoD on the proposals to dispose of the St Lucia site through the usual disposal procedures.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the impact of SR 2024/99 The Registered Rents (Increase) Order (Northern Ireland) 2024, which permits rents of statutory and protected tenancies to be increased by 7.7 per cent.
answer_text: As a result of the 7.7 per cent uplift, there will be an average weekly increase of £6.03 and this will result in an average weekly rent of £84.28. The increase will apply to 334 tenancies. These protected and statutory tenancies already have low rents; comparable to Housing Executive properties, whose rents have also been increased by 7.7%, and substantially lower than that for other private rented sector properties.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any discussions that have taken place between his Department and the Council for Catholic Maintained Schools in relation to increasing the admission and enrollment numbers at Dean Maguirc College, Carrickmore.
answer_text: On 22 March 2024 I announced the outcome of the Normalisation pilot process undertaken by my Department.  The process was designed to ‘Normalise’ approved admissions and enrolment numbers in schools where there was evidence demonstrating a historical pattern of enrolment in excess of a school’s approved numbers.  Dean Maguirc College was initially identified as meeting the criteria and was therefore involved in the process, however, it was not subsequently included in the schools approved to be Normalised.The Council for Catholic Maintained Schools (CCMS) and the school have been advised an appropriate pathway for an increase in the school’s approved numbers is via the Development Proposal (DP) process under Article 14 of the Education and Libraries (Northern Ireland) order 1986.Since my announcement on the outcome of the process, there have been  informal discussions between my officials and the  CCMS in relation to Dean Maguirc College.  I can confirm that formal meetings have been scheduled to take place on 9 and 10 May 2024.Separately, over recent years my Department has approved additional places at the school on an annual basis through the Temporary Variation procedure.  My Department has sought the views of CCMS, via email correspondence, on each of these requests prior to making a decision.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 11012/22-27, for his assessment on the report's suggestion that using Free School Meal Entitlement over the previous six years would be a more accurate assessment of need and deprivation than the current system.
answer_text: There is no cost associated with the report’s main recommendations that DE should retain free school meal entitlement (FSME) as its primary indicator of socioeconomic disadvantage and continue the practice of supplementing this measure, where feasible, with additional indicators of socioeconomic disadvantage. The report recommended that further investigation should be carried out into using an expanded FSME measure in place of the current/past year FSME measure, such as FSME ever6, which measures if a pupil has been entitled to free school meals at any time over the last six years. Preliminary analysis of the FSME ever6 measure indicates that changing to this measure would represent a large increase to the number of pupils considered in the allocation of funding for tackling educational disadvantage. In the context of significant funding pressures this has not been costed. Without an increase in funding available, such a change would expand the number of eligible children, thereby reducing the amount allocated per pupil.  The report suggested it should be a priority to further develop means of securely sharing data across governmental departments in Northern Ireland, for both research and operational purposes. This has not been costed as changes to legislation may be required to facilitate routine cross departmental sharing of data for operational purposes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 11012/22-27, how much implementing the recommendations of this report would cost his Department.
answer_text: There is no cost associated with the report’s main recommendations that DE should retain free school meal entitlement (FSME) as its primary indicator of socioeconomic disadvantage and continue the practice of supplementing this measure, where feasible, with additional indicators of socioeconomic disadvantage. The report recommended that further investigation should be carried out into using an expanded FSME measure in place of the current/past year FSME measure, such as FSME ever6, which measures if a pupil has been entitled to free school meals at any time over the last six years. Preliminary analysis of the FSME ever6 measure indicates that changing to this measure would represent a large increase to the number of pupils considered in the allocation of funding for tackling educational disadvantage. In the context of significant funding pressures this has not been costed. Without an increase in funding available, such a change would expand the number of eligible children, thereby reducing the amount allocated per pupil.  The report suggested it should be a priority to further develop means of securely sharing data across governmental departments in Northern Ireland, for both research and operational purposes. This has not been costed as changes to legislation may be required to facilitate routine cross departmental sharing of data for operational purposes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he plans to incorporate the UN Convention on the Rights of People with Disabilities into law, as recommended by the Equality Commission.
answer_text: Ensuring that disabled people have the same rights and opportunities as the rest of our community is a key priority for me. I am considering the next steps to be taken in the development of an Executive Disability Strategy. This will include consideration of the need for a review and reform of disability legislation and the opportunity that would provide to incorporate the UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of (i) the £875,000 underspend by Sport NI, which equates to 7.9 per cent of its budget; and (ii) Sport NI not highlighting this underspend through its in-year monitoring processes.
answer_text: My Department, is disappointed that Sport NI was unable to make use of its full allocation of funding in 2022/23.   The Department relies on the accuracy of financial information provided by Sport NI. The Board of Sport NI and the management team have taken action in the 2023/24 year to improve financial management and controls and are satisfied that the resource consumption and monitoring process are more effectively managed including the accuracy of financial returns provided to my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the waiting list for mental health services in the Western Health and Social Care Trust in the last three years, broken down by (i) time spent on the waiting list; and (ii) age profile.
To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on his plans to plant additional trees in West Tyrone.
answer_text: I am committed to increasing the current level of tree planting in Northern Ireland. This will make a valuable contribution to climate change mitigation and provide increased environmental and economic benefits for local communities. I have no plans that aim to plant additional trees specifically in West Tyrone; however, my Department’s afforestation programme seeks to encourage landowners, and provides grant aid, to plant new woodlands, including in West Tyrone.I understand that the proportion of land area under woodland in West Tyrone is already higher than the NI average and I welcome the fact that four landowners have recently taken up offers of grant aid to plant new woodlands in the area this year.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the approval of the business case for the Strabane Public Realm Scheme.
answer_text: The Department of Finance has not received a business case relating to the Strabane Public Realm Scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the implications for the Curraghinalt Project (Dalradian) Public Inquiry following his Written Ministerial Statement of 29 April 2024.
answer_text: I believe it is important to continue to provide a forum for interested parties to have their representations considered on the proposed gold mine, given the significant level of public interest in the proposal and the environmental sensitivity of the location.  To this end, I have agreed that my Department should direct the Appeals Commission to consider representations on the two new applications, made in the same terms as the previous, which have been received from the applicant on the 24th April 2024. The representations previously made in relation to the applications for water abstraction, will be made available to the Appeals Commission, in addition to any new representations. It will be for the Appeals Commission to decide how  these new applications will be considered.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs
