answer_text: Thank you for your AQW 8433 / 22-27 dated 7th March 2024.AQW 6750 / 22-27 was answered on 11th March 2024.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of whether the £3.8 million grant allocated by his Department is sufficient to support students facing financial hardship.
answer_text: Each year the Department makes available £2.8m of support funding to local higher education institutions for distribution to students in financial hardship. I have allocated a further £1m of funding in line with the demand projected by the higher education institutions, this demand will continue to be reviewed on an ongoing basis.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how his Department will be supporting mental health initiatives.
answer_text: Infrastructure has an important role to play in improving mental health and I am committed to ensuring we contribute fully to this agenda. Good transport infrastructure and links can enhance social inclusion and connections which contribute to good mental health.  In addition, facilitating active travel also contributes to good physical and mental health.  I recently had a productive meeting with the Mental Health Champion at which I renewed my commitment to supporting Professor O’Neill in her important work.My Department is also represented on the Protect Life 2 Strategy Steering Group, chaired by the Chief Medical Officer. This Group is part of a multi-agency approach to Mental Well-Being, Resilience and Suicide Prevention.I am also keen to support all staff within my Department. The Department provides a number of NICS initiatives through the Departmental Well Champion including Health & Wellbeing sessions, which cover areas including Mental Health.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how he plans to build on the success of the recent increase in the growth in tourism from the South.
answer_text: It is hugely encouraging that more people from the south of Ireland are travelling to the north to holiday. The CSO’s recent Household Travel Survey shows that between January and September last year 1.1 million overnight trips were taken by southern residents to the north. Just nine months into 2023 we have already surpassed the figures for the whole of 2022. Spend rose to £221 million and the number of nights spent in the north rose to 2.5 million. Separate research carried out by Tourism NI has also confirmed this trend. As these figures prove, tourism is a flourishing part of the all-Ireland economy and I am considering how best to build on this success as part of the tourism strategy.I have strong concerns that the British Government’s introduction of its Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) Scheme could threaten these positive numbers as it will act as a disincentive to travel to the north. I have met with the NI Tourism Alliance to discuss this vital issue and to hear at first hand the industry’s concerns. I intend to raise this matter with the Home Office to try to find a solution that protects our tourism industry.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how many students facing financial hardship have been supported by his Department, in each of the last three years.
answer_text: In addition to the financial support provided through student loans, maintenance grants and other targeted support grants, my Department has provided more than £15m of higher education support funds over the last three complete academic years; supporting 8,042 students in 2020/21, 5,265 students in 2021/22 and 5,364 students in 2022/23.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) car parking charges; and (ii) other charges for using the facilities at each country park.
answer_text: Charges at Country Parks, which are all managed by the Northern Ireland Environment Agency (NIEA) are detailed below: There are no car parking charges at any NIEA Country Parks There are a large number of events and activities facilitated across NIEA Country Parks each year which are organised by external organisations. These are usually not charged for unless organisers are themselves charging participants, where organisers do charge then a levy a charge of between 15%-25% of the amount participants are being charged may be applied.Meeting rooms are available to hire at Crawfordsburn Country Park, Peatlands Park and Castle Archdale Country Park.  Charges are detailed in the table below;Room HirePer HourHalf DayFull DayFamily and community/social groups£20£40£80Corporate and commercial businesses£40n/an/aNon-DAERA government groups (including local government), environmental groups and registered charitiesn/a£40£80Schoolsn/a£20£40Environmental NGOs may be allocated up to 3 free uses per year. Staff charges may also apply for out of normal opening hours requests if these can be accommodated.Organised wedding photography events incur a standard charge of £40. Commercial photography, filming and radio recording are subject to individual negotiation.Charges for Electric Car charging at Country Parks where available (managed by a private company) are currently; 7KW Charger30p connection charge + 39p/KwH22KW Charger30p connection charge + 44p/KwH
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what his Department is doing to address poor mental health in the homeless population.
answer_text: The issues of homelessness, involvement with the justice system, mental health and substance use are all inter-related and span the responsibilities of several government departments in Northern Ireland. Therefore, to be effective, our response must be a collective one.My officials continue to participate in the Homeless Strategy Steering Group, alongside other departments, Northern Ireland Housing Executive (NIHE), Public Health Agency, Northern Ireland Federation of Housing Associations and representatives across the voluntary and community sector. This group oversees the implementation plan for the recommendations contained within the NIHE Homelessness Strategy 2022-2027 and develops an annual Inter-departmental Homeless Action Plan (IDHAP) to complement the Strategy. This action plan focuses on addressing gaps in those non-accommodation services that have the most impact, or have the potential to more positively impact, on the lives and life chances of people who are homeless and those who are most at risk of homelessness.Within this year’s IDHAP, my Department is progressing an action to promote mental health through early intervention and prevention. A three-year ‘Early Intervention and Prevention Plan’ has been developed under the Mental Health Strategy and was published in 2023. One of the key actions within this plan is to ensure social determinants and tackling inequalities with an emphasis on deprivation-based inequalities, are key priorities. Officials continue engagement to take forward action plans in relation to the ongoing implementation of the Mental Health Strategy to take into consideration how we continue to tackle mental health for all, including those who are experiencing homelessness.In addition, my Department is represented on the cross-departmental Housing First Task and Finish Group to scope the wider implementation of the Housing First model across Northern Ireland to address chronic homelessness and the complex and intersecting physical and mental health needs of those experiencing it.Further to this, the Belfast Inclusion Health Service provides a wide range of services to those experiencing homelessness, including mental health support, therapeutic work and signposting to appropriate pathways for further support.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what his Department is doing to address hidden homelessness.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Housing Executive has statutory responsibility for responding to homelessness, including provision of homelessness accommodation and has provided me the following information.The Housing Executive’s approach to addressing homelessness is guided by the Homelessness Strategy 2022-27 https://www.nihe.gov.uk/getattachment/73313718-aa0e-4aae-b122-6573dcab88c7/Ending-Homelessness-Together-Homelessness-Strategy-2022-27.pdf The Strategy recognises the collaborative approach required to address homelessness prevention, including addressing hidden homelessness, with more ‘upstream’ interventions such as health and education programmes. The Homelessness Strategy 2022-27 is guided by the development of annual action plans with the Year 2 Action Plan https://www.nihe.gov.uk/getattachment/f82e2297-a070-4aad-ad8e-bdb6fc9668c9/HS-10-09-23-JG-Homelessness-Strategy-2022-27-Year-2-Action-Plan-Accessible.pdf containing a number of actions which seek to increase awareness of homelessness, with such actions including:NIHE will target Homelessness Prevention via provision of awareness sessions to the Community Sector.NIHE will seek to deliver a public awareness campaign to shift mind-sets towards and raise awareness of homelessness prevention. This will include support and promotion of Northern Ireland’s Homelessness Awareness Week.NIHE will explore, and seek to develop, methods by which we can raise awareness of homelessness in the Education sector.The delivery of the Strategy is supported by a number of Homelessness Local Area Groups which seek to improve collaboration at a local level and address any challenges within a specific area, particularly with regards to raising awareness of services. Furthermore, the Housing Executive is developing a Homelessness Strategy Year 3 Action Plan that will seek to build on the actions above and consider any additional actions that will improve awareness of homelessness and address the challenges associated with hidden homelessness. This action plan is currently being developed with a range of partners and subject to approval, will be published in the first quarter of 2024/25.The Housing Executive also recognises the long-term importance of addressing hidden homelessness by focusing on our young people and as part of the Homelessness Strategy has also developed a Youth Homelessness Action Plan which, subject to approval, will be published in the first quarter of 2024/25.I would encourage anyone who finds themselves homeless or in danger of becoming homeless to present themselves to the nearest Housing Executive office which will enable their details to be recorded and appropriate advice and assistance to be offered.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of how the UK's withdrawal from the EU has affected touring and the movement of cultural goods and artists across Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Arts Council of Northern Ireland has worked with its UK counterparts to support the people and organisations in the culture sector, most directly impacted by the UK withdrawal from the EU. It provides important bespoke advice on visa requirements and the movement of goods to and from the UK through its participation in Arts InfoPoint UK. The creation of the Four Nations group of Arts Councils has also brought focus on enabling creative engagement with neighbours in Europe. The two main programmes created through this have been the Four Nations Fund and Cultural Bridges.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities by what date he plans to introduce a ban on no fault evictions.
answer_text: My Department is already progressing work to improve security of tenure through the introduction of much longer notice to quit periods, and I currently have no plans to introduce a ban on no fault evictions.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer AQW 5629/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 5629/22-27 was answered on 10 April 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what plans he has to improve the legislative base of the General Teaching Council NI to deal with regulation.
answer_text: The Department proposes to address current legislative weaknesses in the General Teaching Council for Northern Ireland’s (GTCNI’s) powers to regulate teachers as part of a single Bill which will also address the failings within the body identified by the Effectiveness Review of GTCNI’s Council, undertaken in 2021.Preparatory work for this legislation is well underway and the Department expects to seek Executive approval for the drafting of the Bill before the summer recess.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 7176/22-27, whether he will consider allowing GB-based charity lottery operators to be able to run their existing lottery in Northern Ireland, something that is currently unclear under the 1985 Order.
answer_text: Nothing in Part IV of the Betting, Gaming, Lotteries and Amusements (NI) Order 1985 prohibits GB-based charity lotteries from operating in Northern Ireland, provided they comply with Northern Ireland legislative requirements under Articles 135 – 139 of that Order.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) how many staff vacancies there are in his Department; and (ii) what plans he has to improve the recruitment and retention of staff.
answer_text: As of 4th March 2024, my department had 792 declared vacancies.A recruitment pause in 2023/24 linked to the NICS financial position has impacted our staffing position.  More recently with the lifting of the recruitment pause, some new supply has become available.   My department works closely with DoF to inform the NICS recruitment policy and to request that suitable supply is available as required to meet demand. My officials are also engaging proactively with DoF to inform development of both the NICS People Action Plan for 2024/25 and the NICS People Strategy 2025-2030.In addition, my Department’s bespoke workforce planning processes are enabling us to consider our current and future demand for people, as well as the skills our workforce will need.  This information informs both recruitment and the learning/development actions that will empower our existing workforce to meet current and future business needs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he plans to bring forward legislation to lower the age at which workers are automatically enrolled in a workplace pension from 22 to 18.
answer_text: Subject to NI Executive approval, it is my intention to introduce the Pensions (Extension of Automatic Enrolment) Bill in the coming months.  The Bill, once enacted, will provide my Department with the power to make regulations to reduce the lower age limit at which employees must be automatically enrolled into a pension scheme by their employers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, following the UK government announcement for England and Wales, whether he plans to abolish Work Capability Assessments.
answer_text: In Transforming Support, The Health and Disability White Paper published in March 2023, the UK Government proposed abolishing the Work Capability Assessment.  The White Paper states that primary legislation would be introduced in a new Parliament (after the next general election) when parliamentary time allows.  After that, the changes would be introduced on a staged basis over at least three years beginning no earlier than 2026/27. In general, Northern Ireland’s social security policy and legislation operates in line with the Department for Work and Pensions in Great Britain, and there is, in effect, a single system of social security in place.  It is anticipated therefore that any changes to social security legislation will also be introduced here subject, as necessary, to Ministerial, Executive and Assembly approvals.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice how many Domestic Violence and Abuse Disclosure Scheme applications have been made since they were introduced.
answer_text: From its commencement on 26 March 2018 until 31 January 2024, data from the PSNI shows that there have been a total of 1603 applications made to the Domestic Violence and Abuse Disclosure Scheme. 1358 applications were received via the ‘Right to Ask’ pathway and 245 were received through the ‘Power to Tell’ pathway.  The ‘Right to Ask’ pathway allows a person (or someone they know) who is worried that they may be at risk of domestic abuse from their partner or ex-partner to apply to the scheme. The ‘Power to Tell’ pathway is instigated when a police officer or staff member makes an internal application following receipt of information that a person may be at risk. All applications are processed by the PSNI’s Public Protection Branch who have responsibility for the operation of the scheme.The figures of ‘Right to Ask’ and ‘Power to Tell’ applications may slightly deviate by the end of the financial year. This is because some ‘Right to Ask’ applications are still being processed, and should an applicant disengage from the process and a disclosure is deemed necessary,  the application will be reclassified as a ‘Power to Tell’ application.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice what further actions she will be taking to raise public awareness of the (i) Victim Charter; and (ii) Witness Charter.
answer_text: On 25 February 2024, I launched a public awareness campaign to raise visibility of both the Victim Charter and the Witness Charter.  The campaign will run across television, digital channels and social media until the end of March 2024.  There will also be advertisements in the three main local newspapers. The campaign is in partnership with the Commissioner Designate for Victims of Crime.  As Minister of Justice, I will continue to do what I can to raise awareness of the Charters so that victims and witnesses know where to turn for the support and information they need.This public awareness campaign builds on work that my Department has been leading on over the past year to raise the profile of the Charters.  This has included:continued engagement on social media;promotion of the Charters at festivals such as Mela and Pride; and the development and delivery of awareness sessions to the voluntary and community sector.This work is continuing, and my officials are intending to provide presentations on the Charters to various groups in the coming months.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he has considered using the model used in Scotland to fund community pharmacists to help them maintain and improve their supply of medicines.
answer_text: I can assure the member that my Department is continuing to examine ways to maintain and improve the supply of medicines including examples elsewhere such as Scotland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to list the number of successful Right to Buy applications that were received by the Housing Executive in each of the last 5 years.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows.“The table below outlines the number of completed tenant purchases under the House Sales scheme in each of the last 5 years.Financial YearTotal2019/204482020/212392021/224442022/235542023/24355Total2040”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the end to the Right to Buy scheme for the Housing Executive.
answer_text: I have no plans to bring forward legislation to end the Northern Ireland Housing Executive’s House Sales Scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) whether he has received a copy of the report commissioned by Translink regarding the impacts on the rail network from climate change, and (ii) what action his Department plans to take to help mitigate these impacts.
answer_text: Translink has submitted a copy of the ‘Sea Defences - Sea Level & Climate Change Assessment’ report, which it commissioned. The report forms part of the overall risk assessment of railway assets across the network and will be used to contribute to a climate resilience strategy to protect the railway network.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what alternative funding routes are available for the ten building projects for shared and integrated schools that had their funding withdrawn, including Drumragh Integrated College, Omagh.
answer_text: I have placed the 10 remaining former Fresh Start projects that are not yet in contract into my Department’s conventional major works programme to continue to advance in design and planning.  The projects will proceed to construction when planning is complete and affordable within the wider education budget context.All of the projects have been submitted as capital bids for 2024-25 to the Department of Finance and I trust all Executive parties will support this bid for capital funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the Strabane Public Realm Scheme.
answer_text: The Strabane Public Realm Scheme is at business case stage and is currently scheduled for casework committee consideration within my Department.  Following the review by the committee, the project will require my approval as well as approval from the Department of Finance.  I will give due consideration to funding this project, alongside other capital priorities across my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the cost to bring the road network up to an acceptable condition.
answer_text: My Department’s Resource budget, which is used to deliver day-to-day maintenance activities such as patching and grass cutting, has been under significant pressure for a number of years. As a result, my Department has had to reduce its routine road maintenance activities to a limited service, which prioritises delivery of essential services for maintaining public safety and protecting the transport network. In 2023-24, it was estimated that c.£55-60m would be required to deliver normal Essential Maintenance service levels. However, allocations of funding to maintain our roads have been less than required for many years and my Department has relied on in-year monitoring rounds to try to fund a limited version of essential maintenance and also our Winter Service.My Department also places high importance on the need to adequately maintain the road network through a properly resourced programme of structural maintenance. The purpose of the programme is to maintain the integrity of the network. Such maintenance includes strengthening, surface dressing, patching and drainage works. It has been independently established that some £192 million (at 22/23 prices) is needed to maintain the structural integrity of the North’s road network.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) to detail the number of (a) British Sign Language (BSL); and (b) Irish Sign Language (ISL) interpreters in Northern Ireland; and (ii) what plans he has to increase these numbers, and by how many.
answer_text: There are currently thirty-five qualified, registered BSL interpreters and three qualified, registered ISL interpreters in Northern Ireland.My Department is funding the Foyle Deaf Association, a Deaf-led Sign Language Teaching Centre in Londonderry, to deliver an Interpreter Training Programme to 6 BSL students and 6 ISL students to attain accredited qualifications and registration to practise as registered interpreters. My officials are also working with the Centre for Translation and Interpreting at Queen’s University Belfast to provide an academic route to qualification as an interpreter to further increase capacity and expertise, and to future-proof a sustainable supply of interpreters.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the rationale for referring Members seeking a ministerial response via an Assembly Written Question to the Public Affairs Unit at the Housing Executive.
answer_text: This approach was adopted to ensure clarity between the Department’s policy responsibilities and Northern Ireland Housing Executive’s (NIHE) operational remit, as well as to ensure that MLAs receive accurate, timely and full responses.Following the Speaker’s comments in Assembly plenary on 12 March 2024, I will seek operational information from the NIHE Chief Executive and provide it in response to Assembly Questions.Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities who makes a decision on whether the subject of an Assembly Written Question is an operational matter.
answer_text: Following the Speaker’s comments in Assembly plenary on 12 March 2024, I will seek operational information from the Chief Executives of my Department’s Arm’s Length Bodies and provide it in response to Assembly Questions.Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when Stage 2 of changes to the Housing Selection Scheme will be introduced.
answer_text: Work is ongoing to implement proposals within the second phase of the Fundamental Review of Allocations project.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how he intends to rationalise the arm’s length bodies in his Department, as set out in New Decade, New Approach.
answer_text: In line with the New Decade, New Approach commitment my Department has commissioned reviews for three of its Arm’s Length Bodies so far - the Northern Ireland Commissioner for Children and Young People (NICCY), the Commissioner for Older People for Northern Ireland (COPNI) and the Northern Ireland Local Government Officers Superannuation Committee (NILGOSC).  Consideration will be given to further reviews in line with the New Decade, New Approach commitment.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs how much the company involved in the Meenbog landslide has contributed to the clean up.
answer_text: On 7th June 2022, the company involved were prosecuted in Letterkenny District Court and ordered to pay a fine of €1500 and to repay Loughs Agency's costs for restoration. These costs amounted to £27,000  and have since been reimbursed.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he plans to meet the Ulster Poultry Federation to discuss their concerns in relation to regulations introduced since Brexit.
answer_text: My office has recently received correspondence from the Chairperson of the Ulster Poultry Federation requesting a meeting in relation to concerns around regulations introduced since EU exit.  	My officials will contact the Federation Chair in the coming days with a view to agreeing a meeting date to discuss these matters.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he plans to introduce new animal welfare legislation.
answer_text: I am acutely aware of the need for animal welfare reform across a range of areas. I will be engaging with my officials and stakeholders in the time ahead to establish priorities and to determine what legislation can be delivered within the lifetime of the current assembly.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he plans to fund the Skill Up initiative after the end of March 2024.
answer_text: The Skill Up programme is currently being evaluated and I will consider any new programme as part of the upcoming Budget exercise.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he plans to review his Department's Wind Energy Development guidance.
answer_text: The guidance 'Wind Energy Development in Northern Ireland Landscapes' published on August 2010, is the responsibility of DAERA.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of small businesses that have ceased trading, broken down by local council, for each of the last three years.
answer_text: My Department does not hold the exact information requested.NISRA’s Inter-Departmental Business Register includes information on business de-registrations. However, this does not identify whether a business has specifically ceased trading, or if it has merely deregistered a Value Added Tax (VAT) account and/or a Pay As You Earn (PAYE) scheme.NISRA’s most recently published Business Demography data relates to the year ending December 2022. A full suite of tables is available on the NISRA website, with Table 1.6 providing a breakdown by local government district for 2017 to 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he plans to (i) review; and (ii) change the provisions of the Insolvency (Northern Ireland) Order 1989.
answer_text: I will be discussing insolvency legislation with my officials and formulating my approach over the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, further to the Motor Vehicles (Compulsory Insurance) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022, for an update on his work to reduce insurance costs for vehicles.
answer_text: I am aware of the rising cost of vehicle insurance and the challenges this is causing for our road users. A recent report by the Association of British Insurers has concluded that the rising premiums are due to the increased costs for insurance companies, with repair costs being the largest single factor. The factors identified as causing the rise in insurance premiums, being commercial, are outside the remit of my Department. However, the work carried out to mitigate against uninsured driving, improve road safety awareness, and expand the road maintenance programme, all help to reduce the number of incidents on the road and, in turn, keep premiums as low as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he plans to review the rules regarding the issuing of parking tickets to blue badge holders in off-street car parks.
answer_text: The ownership and management of publicly provided off-street car parks transferred from the Department to local councils on 1 April 2015, making the issuing of parking tickets to Blue Badge holders a matter for local councils.The off-street parking places operated by the Department are “Park and Ride” facilities and there are no plans to review the rules regarding the issuing of parking tickets to Blue Badge holders in these locations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the turnaround time it takes from the application for a blue badge to the issuing of the badge.
answer_text: Blue Badge applicants will generally be informed of their entitlement within 6 to 8 weeks of the date of the receipt of their application. However, if more information is required to support their application from their doctor it may take longer to issue a decision.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will answer AQW 5243/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 5243/22-27 was answered on 7th March 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will answer AQW 4655/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 4655 22-27 was answered on 7th March 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will answer AQW 4656/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 4656/22-27 was answered on 7th March 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 5245/22-27.
answer_text: My apologies for the delay in responding.  AQW 5245 asked to detail the level of vacant (i) doctor; and (ii) nursing posts, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust area.  Information on HSC vacancies actively being recruited to by staff group, is published by my Department on a quarterly basis, at the link below.https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/articles/staff-vacanciesThe latest information on the number of HSC doctor and registered nursing vacancies actively being recruited to at 31 December 2023, in each Health and Social Care (HSC) Trust, is detailed in the following table.HSC TrustHSC Vacancies actively being recruited to at 31 December 2023DoctorsRegistered NursesBelfast79357Northern29244South Eastern 33248Southern57200Western90195Source: Recruitment & Selection Shared Services (BSO) & HSC Trusts

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will answer AQW 5244/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 5244 22-27 was answered on 7th March 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the pay and grading review for Non-Teaching Support Staff in the Education Authority, including provision for resourcing.
answer_text: My officials are currently working with the Education Authority to urgently address queries in relation to the Pay & Grading review business case.  I have asked them to complete this work as quickly as possible as I am committed to ensuring that support staff in the education sector are paid at a fair level for the important work they do. There are significant costs associated with the Pay & Grading review and my Department will need additional funding from the Executive in order to implement its recommendations.  Following the agreement of the financial package from HM Treasury the Finance Minister allocated additional Resource DEL funding of £296.8m to the Department of Education.  This did not include any funding in respect of the EA Pay & Grading review for education sector support staff.  However, the Finance Minister has advised that this issue can be revisited as part of the Budget 2024-25 process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he plans to reopen the Boiler Replacement Scheme for new applications.
answer_text: I refer the member to my response to AQW 6960 22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) to detail his Department’s policy regarding engagement with community groups; and (ii) whether he plans to review this policy to improve this engagement.
answer_text: My Department engages with community groups on a regular basis including during policy development and consultations. The Department follows the principles and processes of engagement with stakeholder groups, including community groups, which is outlined in ‘Making a Difference -The NICS Guide To Making Policy That Works’ and the Equality Commission for Northern Ireland’s Section 75 Equality Screening and Assessments advice.  To assist the Department in reaching the widest possible audience during a public consultation or when conducting a full Equality Impact Assessment consultation my Department informs all of those consultees included in its Section 75 list. This list includes a wide range of community and voluntary organistions and this list is updated regularly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the waiting list for extensions to Housing Executive homes, broken down by constituency; and (ii) the length of time applicants have been waiting.
answer_text: The waiting list for extensions to Housing Executive homes is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. 	You should, therefore, direct your question to the Housing Executive at its dedicated MLA response team, which can be contacted at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he plans to enable large charity lotteries to operate in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Charity lottery operations in Northern Ireland are regulated under Part IV of the Betting, Gaming, Lotteries and Amusements (NI) Order 1985 (‘the 1985 Order’).  Nothing in Part IV of the 1985 Order prohibits large charity lotteries from operating in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance what discussions she has held with Treasury in relation to facilitating a one-off carryover of funding, given our unique circumstances in this financial year.
answer_text: The financial package accompanying the returning Executive provides £1045.6 million Resource DEL for 2023-24. This funding is only available for 2032-24. As this funding is treated as a reserve claim it is ineligible for carry forward.All other categories of expenditure are subject to carryover as usual under the Budget Exchange Scheme.As part of the Westminster Supplementary Estimate process, the Executive received additional Barnett consequentials for 2023-24 of £41.553 million RDEL and £1.634 million CDEL for which I sought carryover. Due to the late stage in the year, Treasury has agreed this funding can be carried over in 2024-25.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance what assurances individual Ministers have given her to ensure that funding allocated to Departments is able to be spent, so that no money is returned to Treasury.
answer_text: ANSWERAt its meeting on 15 February, the Executive agreed the final departmental funding envelopes for the 2023-24 financial year, utilising the additional £1046.5 million from the financial package. The funding has been provided as a total Resource DEL allocation for each department to allow individual Ministers to manage their budgets effectively. It is the responsibility of individual departmental Ministers to ensure that they live within the funding envelopes provided and avoid both overspends and underspends.At the meeting and in the paper agreed by Executive colleagues on 15 February I outlined the consequences of underspends by departments and how these would result in funding being lost to the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what action he will take to ensure that school budgets have sufficient resources to continue to meet the wages of their staff in full in the new financial year.
answer_text: As you are aware, Department budgets for 2024-25 have not yet been set.  Following the recent agreement of the financial package from HM Treasury, my Department was allocated additional funding to contribute towards settling three years of outstanding non-contractual Teaching pay awards in 2023-24. The Department cannot be expected to manage the recurring impact of settling these pay awards, which is estimated at c£166m, without additional baselined funding in the 2024-25 financial year and beyond.Given that approximately 80% of the Education budget relates to paying teachers, classroom assistants and other vital support staff, I will be strongly making the case to my Executive colleagues that Education will require substantially more funding during the incoming budget period, if we are to avoid a significant and detrimental impact on our children and young people.I do however recognise the need to provide some clarity for schools as soon as possible and therefore budgetary planning is currently being undertaken within my Department, pending the agreement of a Final Budget, to facilitate some reasonable planning to proceed in the meantime.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance what action she will take to bridge the £12million shortfall she identifies to fund pay settlements in the public sector.
answer_text: The financial package accompanying the return of the Executive provides £1045.6 million in 2023-24.  Ideally the financial package would have provided funding to meet all pay demands this year and cover future on-going costs, however this is not the case.The financial package which accompanied the return of the Executive, while welcome, does not put the Executive’s finances on a sustainable footing. I have a number of significant concerns in regard to the financial package and have written to the Chief Secretary outlining these. I intend to raise these when I meet with the Treasury this week.At its meeting on 15 February, the Executive agreed allocations to departments to address pay and forecast overspends using the funding available this year. It is now for individual Ministers to manage pay awards within the funding envelopes provided.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for a breakdown of the per capita Arts Council funding (i) by local council area; (ii) in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The distribution of funding is an operational matter for the Arts Council of Northern Ireland. You should, therefore, direct your question to the Communications Team of the Arts Council of Northern Ireland at info@artscouncil-ni.org.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of wind turbines in each constituency, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The Department does not hold or compile the information you have requested. It is advised that you seek this information from the relevant council planning authorities.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the time it took from application to planning decision to be made for each regionally significant wind turbine application, in the last 10 years.
answer_text: The following planning applications categorised as regionally significant wind farm developments  were determined within the time period stipulated:LA10/2015/0292/F	Doraville Wind Farm 	- 265 weeksLA09/2016/0232/F 	Corlacky Wind Farm 	- 240 weeks

answer_text: The tables below provide the details of regionally significant wind farm applications and pre-consultation submissions (Pre-Application Notices (PANs) and Pre-Application Discussions (PADs)) submitted to my Department by parliamentary constituency in each of the last three years.Individual wind turbine applications are not deemed regionally significant and therefore none are recorded nor have been submitted to my Department in the last three years.Regionally Significant Applications Received - April 2020 - September 2023 by parliamentary constituencyEast AntrimNorth AntrimWest Tyrone2020/202112021/202212022/20231April-Sept 2023*1Regionally Significant Pre-Application Notices (PAN) Received - April 2020 - September 2023 by parliamentary constituencyEast AntrimNorth AntrimWest Tyrone2020/20212021/20221112022/20231April-Sept 2023*1Regionally Significant Pre-Application Discussions (PAD) Received - April 2020 - September 2023 by parliamentary constituencyEast AntrimNorth AntrimWest Tyrone2020/20212021/202212022/2023April-Sept 2023** Data for April - September 2023 is provisional.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he intends to review the Schools for the future - A policy for sustainable schools, and,  in particular, the minimum threshold of 105 pupils for rural primary schools.
answer_text: My departmental officials are currently considering the recommendations of the Independent Review of Education panel, including those in relation to the minimum enrolment thresholds outlined in the Schools for the Future – A Policy for Sustainable Schools (SSP), and will report to me on this by the end of April 2024. I will consider the need to commission a full review of SSP after I have considered that report.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on all capital works planned for GP practices in West Tyrone.
answer_text: A number of projects have recently been completed, are under way or are planned in the West Tyrone constituency area to create additional capacity within GP practices.  These include:Omagh Primary Care Complex - capital works completed in the past 12 months.Western Rural Health Care (Newtownstewart) – capital works currently underway and due to complete in the coming months.Strabane Health and Care Centre - business case currently being developed in relation to a new purpose-built hub in Strabane, as part of the Derry & Strabane City Deal.Carrickmore Health Centre - currently exploring options to meet short-term practice needs.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 6071/22-27, to list the number of cases when the NIEA has taken more than six months to respond to planning consultation requests.
answer_text: DAERA responds to planning application consultations through a single collated response covering NIEA and DAERA Marine and Fisheries Division.  Response rates are therefore collated and reported at a Departmental level.  Since 1 April 2023, the Department has receipted 3,336 Statutory planning consultations. Of these consultations, responses have issued for 16 applications which took more than six months to respond to.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for a timeline for all Housing Executive refurbishment work planned across West Tyrone constituency in the next 12 months.
answer_text: Housing Executive refurbishment works planned across West Tyrone are an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. You should therefore direct your question to the Northern Ireland Housing Executive at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether there are any plans to modernise Strabane Courthouse.
answer_text: There are currently no specific plans for the modernisation of Strabane Courthouse. In December 2023, the Northern Ireland Courts and Tribunals Service (NICTS) published its Estate Strategy, Making the Place for Justice.https://www.justice-ni.gov.uk/publications/northern-ireland-courts-and-tribunals-service-estate-strategy-2023-2030The Strategy is structured around 4 Improvement Aims and 12 Strategic Outcomes, that if realised, will result in a courts and tribunals estate that is capable of providing modernised and improved facilities for all citizens of Northern Ireland.To implement its Estate Strategy, NICTS will use data and evidence to baseline the existing estate against the agreed Strategic Outcomes, and develop prioritised investment delivery plans in the context of the resources available.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of animal abuse cases (i) reported to his Department; and (ii) prosecuted, in each of the last three years.
answer_text: The table below details the information requested with regard to animal welfare cases reported to and prosecuted by my Department.Under the Welfare of Animals Act (Northern Ireland) 2011, my Department is responsible for enforcing the provisions that relate to farmed animals only.  For the purposes of this response, cases of animal abuse, has been interpreted as animal welfare concerns reported to my Department by members of the public and other authorities, for investigation. 	Year	Number of on-farm animal welfare concerns reported 	Number of inspections where a breach was detected	Number of animal welfare cases prosecuted	2021	124	29	6 (plus 1 formal caution delivered)	2022	139	32	1 (plus 1 formal caution delivered)	2023*	179	39	6	*2023 figures are provisional and yet to be finalised.These figures are published on the DAERA website as part of the annual Animal Welfare Report, which details the action taken by each of the three enforcement bodies with responsibility for animal welfare.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the work his Department is undertaking to increase out-of-hours provision in Strabane town.
answer_text: Western Urgent Care (WUC) is commissioned to provide the GP Out of Hours (OOHs) service across the Western area.  The GP OOHs service is for people who need urgent medical treatment but cannot wait until their doctor's practice reopens. The local population is encouraged to utilise the service appropriately to meet any urgent health care needs which cannot wait to be seen by their registered practice when it reopens.  The GP OOHs service is available Mon-Fri from 6pm to 8am the next morning, as well as 24 hours at weekends and during public holidays.  All Patients can contact WUC via a single telephone number, there are no telephone numbers available to contact bases directly. Patients who contact the service will be triaged by a telephone or video call by clinical staff who could be located in any one of the service’s bases in Derry/ Londonderry, Enniskillen, Limavady, Strabane or Omagh. Many patients can be safely and effectively clinically triaged by either a Nurse Advisor, Prescribing Pharmacist, an Advanced Nurse Practitioners (ANPs) or GP.  The majority of patients who contact WUC OOHs service do not need to be seen face-to-face.  From Apr-Dec 2023, 71% of WUC OOHs patient contacts were given advice by telephone or video call, 25% of patient contacts required a base visit and 4% of contacts required a home visit. The GP OOH service provided by WUC faces considerable challenges in consistently populating the GP rota in Strabane, despite best efforts.  Reasons for this include the fact that GPs are not contractually obliged to work in GP OOH; shifting attitudes in relation to achieving work life balance and preparedness or willingness of doctors to cover out of hours’ sessions; and busier and more complex day time general practice reducing the inclination or ability of GPs to work additional out of hours sessions.  When GPs book to work in out of hours clinics, the majority prefer to work within their local area - close to their own practices and homes.  Base and Home visits for Strabane population:  Strabane GP OOHs base is currently open between 8am and 4pm on weekends and bank holidays with one Clinician, a GP or an Advanced Nurse Practitioner (ANP), working in the Strabane base at a time. WUC have advised this is in response to low numbers of clinicians booking shifts in the Strabane base and has been necessary in order to efficiently utilise the available resources.  Any Patients who require a face to face appointment during the overnight period on weekdays are given the option of attending another base, either Derry/Londondery or Omagh.If there is a request for a home visit and there is no GP in the Strabane base, a GP from another base will make the visit to wherever the patient is located. If at weekends or on Public holidays there is no GP in the Strabane base, and a patient needs to be seen by a GP, they are given the option of attending another base or waiting to be seen at their local base. question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to detail the number of orthopaedic surgeons, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust; (ii) how many orthopaedic surgeons have left employment, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust, in each of the last three years; and (iii) what plans he has for recruiting more orthopaedic surgeons.
To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the planned new build for St Catherine's Primary School, Strabane.
answer_text: St Catherine’s Primary School was announced to advance in planning under the Major Capital Works Programme in June 2020.An Integrated Consultant Team was appointed in October 2021 who will be responsible for the full design of the project and management of the building contractor. A Business Case was approved in June 2023 confirming the preferred option as a new build fourteen class base primary school across the existing sites, with nursery in the refurbished listed building. The project is currently at the Concept Design Stage (RIBA Stage 2), and a Report on this stage is anticipated to be lodged with the Department within the coming weeks. Although still at an early stage and subject to change, current estimates are that construction work would commence on site in January 2026.  However, without additional capital funding this project will be paused when it reaches RIBA Stage 4 (Technical Design and Pre-Tender Estimate) and will not be permitted to progress to the construction stage.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how many social housing properties have benefitted from cavity wall insulation schemes in each of the last 5 years, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: The number of Housing Executive social housing properties which have benefitted from cavity wall insulation schemes in the last 5 years is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.  You should, therefore, direct your question to the Northern Ireland Housing Executive at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk		The Department does not hold information on cavity wall insulation schemes for housing associations.  You should, therefore, seek the information requested from the relevant organisations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what capital investment is planned for St Colmcille's Primary School, Carrickmore.
answer_text: My Department is currently progressing a feasibility report and business case for a minor work scheme to replace three modular classrooms and deliver a traffic management solution at Colmcille's Primary School.  Delivery of the works is subject to the availability of capital funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the additional parking spaces at St Conor's Primary School, Omagh.
answer_text: Two potential options to deliver additional parking spaces within the existing grounds of St Conor’s Primary School were rejected by the school in January 2023.  Whilst my Department had engaged with the Chief Executive of Fermanagh and Omagh District Council regarding the potential to acquire land opposite the school to facilitate additional parking, this work had to be paused, due to the restricted capital budget position in 2023/24.  My Department has not received confirmation of its capital budget for 2024/25 and I am therefore not yet in a position to confirm whether work on the St Conor’s PS traffic management scheme can recommence.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of individuals on the social housing waiting list for ground floor accommodation, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: The number of applicants on the social housing waiting list for ground floor accommodation broken down by constituency is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.You should, therefore, direct your question to the Housing Executive at its dedicated MLA response team, which can be contacted at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how many cases of damp and condensation have been reported to the Housing Executive, for each of the last three years, broken down by local council area.
answer_text: Reports of damp and condensation cases is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. 		You should therefore direct your question to the Northern Ireland Housing Executive at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what additional support he will provide to community pharmacists to maintain and improve their supply of medicines.
answer_text: I very much appreciate the extremely important work that community pharmacies deliver for our population.The funding provided to the sector compares favourably with other parts of the UK.I have received representation from pharmacy contractors and from their representative body, CPNI. An urgent meeting is being arranged to meet with representatives of CPNI. In advance of that meeting, I can assure the member that we are looking into ways to stabilise the sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on all capital projects funded by his Department in West Tyrone in the last 4 years.
answer_text: While it is not possible to dis-aggregate capital spend by constituency, details are provided below of specific capital projects in the West Tyrone constituency.From 2019/20 to 2023/24, capital grant assistance totalling £31.4 million was made towards ‘Gas to the West’, a major infrastructure project taken forward by private sector developers to connect Omagh, Enniskillen, Derrylin, Dungannon, Coalisland, Cookstown, Magherafelt and Strabane to natural gas.  It is not possible to isolate grant specific to particular areas within this project.Over a five-year period to 2024/25, £199 million has been allocated to Project Stratum to provide access to gigabit-capable broadband infrastructure to 81,100 premises.  It is not possible to attribute spending at a constituency level but 9,989 premises in West Tyrone will benefit from Project Stratum.Five projects from the West Tyrone constituency area have benefitted from financial assistance received from Tourism NI for capital projects, totalling £0.1 million since February 2020.  The projects assisted were across a variety of funding programmes including website development and experience development programmes.Between 2019/20 and 2022/23, Invest NI offered assistance of £2.6 million towards capital projects in the West Tyrone constituency. The West Tyrone constituency encompasses South West College’s Omagh Campus and North West Regional College’s Strabane Campus.  My Department has provided capital funding through its ongoing Further Education Minor Works Scheme for upgrades to building fabric, sustainable estate solutions, emerging equipment requirements and to ensure Health and Safety standards are met.
