question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of households in receipt of Universal Credit, broken down by constituency for each of the last three years.
answer_text: At August 2023, a total of 136,520 households were claiming Universal Credit. The published statistics are available online and provide the total number of households and claimants in receipt of Universal Credit since October 2017. The statistics also provide a breakdown of the number of claimants in each constituency:www.communities-ni.gov.uk/articles/universal-credit-statistics

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 5506/22-27, to provide a date by which he will respond to the recommendations in the Independent Review of Education.
answer_text: Officials are aiming to have a paper with me by the end of April 2024.  I will then consider the paper and respond in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he intends to reinstate the Healthy Happy Minds Programme.
answer_text: The Healthy Happy Minds Pilot concluded on 31 March 2023.  An evaluation of the pilot has been carried out and I will assess its findings as I seek to better support the emotional health and wellbeing of learners.  The Independent Counselling Service for Schools (ICSS) remains available to post-primary age pupils.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what measures his Department will put in place to remove blue-green algae, and prevent its spread, from Moorlough Lake, West Tyrone.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Environment Agency, (NIEA) investigated a report of an algal bloom at Moorlough, Strabane on 12 January 2024 and confirmed the presence of blue-green algae. Whilst there are no quick fixes to the occurrence and probable reoccurrence of blue-green algal blooms, my Department is leading a cross departmental Water Quality Steering Group and is in the process of finalising a draft report with a set of evidenced based recommendations to tackle blue-green algae and secure longer-term improvements in water quality across Northern Ireland.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the social housing waiting list in each constituency, for each of the last three years.
answer_text: The social housing waiting list in each constituency for each of the last three years is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.You should, therefore, direct your question to the Housing Executive at its dedicated MLA response team, which can be contacted at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of whether the Education Authority is fit for purpose.
answer_text: My priority is to ensure the delivery of the best possible services for our children and young people throughout the education system. The Landscape Review published in June 2022 identified that some progress has been made within the Education Authority (EA), however it is clear that more work needs to be done. My Department is fully committed to working with the EA to progress the recommendations and to deliver improved services for the children and young people that it serves.My Department is also currently considering the proposals outlined in the Independent Review of Education.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has assessed the effectiveness of the HGV restrictions in Clady village.
answer_text: Officials in my department have received informal feedback from residents who advised that the weight restriction in Clady Village has been considered successful in reducing the number of large Heavy Goods Vehicles passing through the village.  It is planned that a temporary automatic traffic counter will be installed in the coming months to gather information on HGV numbers in the village to seek to quantify the effectiveness of the introduction of the weight restriction.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the average time it takes NIEA to respond to planning consultation requests.
answer_text: DAERA responds to planning application consultations through a single collated response covering NIEA and DAERA Marine and Fisheries Division.  Response rates are therefore collated and reported at a Departmental level.  Since 1 April 2023, the average time taken to respond to statutory planning consultations is 17 days.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of motor neurone disease treatments in Northern Ireland in comparison to Great Britain and the Republic of Ireland.
answer_text: In Northern Ireland, the Managed Entry of New Medicines Process normally ensures that NICE approved treatments can be made available on a cost per case basis once NICE has published a Final Draft Guidance (FDG) which predates final publication by NICE. This practice ensures that NICE approved treatments are often made available to patients in Northern Ireland sooner than in any other part of the UK. In the absence of a decision by NICE, the Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) within my Department will consider the adoption of decisions made by the Scottish Medicines Consortium (SMC) or the All Wales Medicines Strategy Group (AWMSG).Where a request for treatment falls outwith the Managed Entry process, arrangements are in place for the SPPG to consider requests for funding on an individual patient basis in accordance with the DoH Individual Funding Request (IFR) policy.There is no known cure for Motor Neurone Disease (MND), however, NICE has recommended the use of riluzole for the treatment of individuals with the amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) form of MND to slow progression.  This treatment is routinely available in NI. In addition, other drugs commonly used/offered are those prescribed for symptom management.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on her plans to amend sentencing guidelines for those convicted of causing death by careless driving when under the influence of drink or drugs.
answer_text: In October 2019 the Department of Justice launched a wide-ranging consultation on a review of sentencing policy. This included seeking views on increasing the maximum sentence for driving offences causing death or serious injury, which was done with agreement from the Department for Infrastructure, which has responsibility for road traffic policy. Causing death by careless driving while under the influence of drink or drugs falls within those offences. The way forward report from that consultation was published in April 2021 and proposed several changes to current sentences through a planned Sentencing Bill. I remain committed to bring forward legislative provisions to this effect within the remainder of this mandate. The creation and promulgation of sentencing guidelines is a matter for the independent judiciary.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the decision to refuse Planning Application LA11/2018/0186/O, Strabane Athletics' plans for a new multi-sport indoor and outdoor facility.
answer_text: On 27 July 2023, a Notice of Opinion issued to refuse outline planning permission on the grounds that the proposal was contrary to the local development plan, regional policy and road safety and traffic flow.  Derry City and Strabane District Council subsequently wrote to Departmental Planning officials seeking a hearing before the Planning Appeals Commission (PAC). Departmental officials wrote to the PAC to request that the Commission make the necessary arrangements for the Council to appear before and be heard by the Commission. My Department await the PAC to set a date for this.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how he will improve the availability and sustainability of NHS dentists.
answer_text: A key part of the access issues facing patients are the rates being paid to our dentists for treatment.  The absence of Executive Ministers has meant that it has not been possible to progress the recommended pay uplifts for staffing groups for 2023/24.  This is now my immediate priority, and I am meeting with Trade Unions in early course to take this forward. Officials have already been making arrangements in anticipation of funding being made available to ensure that uplifts can be implemented as quickly as possible.I know that an additional £95m has been invested in General Dental Services via the Financial Support Scheme and Rebuilding Support Scheme since April 2020. This came to an end in July 2023 due to the severe financial pressures arising from the Department’s 2023/24 budget settlement.A pilot scheme is currently running to provide appointments to unregistered patients who require emergency dental treatment during the working week. If a patient is not registered with a dentist, requires emergency dental treatment, and has been unable to access an appointment, they can contact their local Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) office for advice.I am also conscious of the progress that has been made in the rest of the UK on dental reform and I will be discussing these issues with officials as soon as possible, to ensure that the sector here in NI is supported in delivering important care to patients. More immediately, officials have been developing investment proposals to further support General Dental Services and I will consider them in early course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on all social housing improvement schemes in West Tyrone, including (i) the nature of the improvement; and (ii) the timeframe for completion.
answer_text: Social housing improvement schemes in West Tyrone are an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. 		You should therefore direct your question to the Northern Ireland Housing Executive at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what progress his Department has made in delivering the recommendations made in A Fair Start: Final Report and Action Plan.
answer_text: Whilst there have been significant budget pressures faced by the Department of Education this year, officials are progressing 45 actions in 2023/24.  The latest 6th Monthly report will be published on the DE website in due course. Previous progress reports can be found at: https://www.education-ni.gov.uk/publications/progress-reports-fair-start-report-and-action-plans

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he will progress the Independent Review of Education recommendation for a Single Education Department.
answer_text: The Department is currently considering the Report produced by the Independent Review Panel and carrying out internal consultation to inform a way forward. This process includes discussion with key education stakeholders including other relevant Departments and Arm’s-Length Bodies.    Following this consultation, officials will produce a paper for my consideration setting out advice on the various recommendations in the report.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will progress the Independent Review of Education recommendation for a Single Education Department.
answer_text: My Department is currently considering the Report and carrying out internal consultation to inform a way forward in conjunction with the Department of Education. This process includes discussion with key education stakeholders including other relevant Departments and Arm’s-Length Bodies.  Following this consultation, officials will produce a paper for my consideration setting out advice on the various recommendations in the report.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the replacement body for the General Teaching Council For Northern Ireland.
answer_text: On 13 December 2021, then Education Minister, Michelle McIlveen MLA, announced in the Assembly her decision to dissolve and replace the General Teaching Council for Northern Ireland (GTCNI), a step requiring new legislation.  She also committed the Department to undertake a public consultation on the form a replacement should take and the functions it should exercise.  This consultation ran from 13 June to 30 September 2022 and an analysis of the responses received was published in October 2022 – Consultation on Functions Delivered by the General Teaching Council for Northern Ireland Analysis of Responses.Subsequently, further targeted engagement with teachers was undertaken, alongside discussions with the other United Kingdom and Republic of Ireland teaching regulators and other key stakeholders.  These all helped to refine the Department’s thinking on an optimal GTCNI replacement; and on 5 February 2024, having previously shared its thinking with our teaching unions, the Department shared with all Northern Ireland’s teachers, its thinking on the key elements to be addressed within a GTCNI replacement Bill.The Department has requested that teachers provide us with feedback on our proposals and, subject to the views received, the Department will continue to develop a GTCNI replacement Bill, and to seek its Assembly introduction at the earliest possible opportunity.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the Education Authority's management of EOTAS provision for the Strabane area during the current school year.
answer_text: Primary EOTAS in the Strabane area is currently delivered from Strabane Youth Centre as an interim accommodation arrangement.  I am aware there are accommodation pressures for such provision as a result of a decision by the Education Authority (EA) to withdraw primary EOTAS provision at Fir Trees to accommodate additional special school nursery placements in Knockavoe Special School.  This decision impacted five children due to be placed at the centre.   The timing of when the decision was communicated to learners, parents and schools was not satisfactory.  My officials have raised this matter with the EA.   Work is ongoing to identify replacement accommodation for Fir Trees EOTAS and is at an advanced stage.Following engagement with staff from each of the affected pupil’s registered mainstream school, bespoke enhanced in-school arrangements have been agreed. These arrangements have been developed according to each individual pupil’s level of need.   This means there is enhanced in-school support for pupils affected by the closure of Fir Trees, in addition to two sessions per week for pupils in Strabane Youth Centre.The Post Primary EOTAS provision continues to operate out of Knockavoe Resource Centre.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he plans to reintroduce the Cross Border Healthcare Directive.
answer_text: Reducing waiting times for elective access is a key priority for me. Current waiting times are unacceptable, no one should have to wait longer than necessary for the surgery they need.  While good progress has been made in tackling waiting times in some areas, there remains much more to be done. Within the constraints of existing budgets, I’m committed to taking whatever actions I can to tackle these waiting times and ensure that patients are treated as quickly as possible to ensure best possible outcomes.As you will be aware, I introduced the Republic of Ireland Reimbursement Scheme (RoIRS) on 30 June 2021 as part of the Elective Care Framework to help reduce NI waiting lists. The scheme was a limited 12-month administrative version of the Cross-Border Healthcare Directive (CBHD) which ceased to apply to NI following the UK’s exit from the EU. In June 2022, I announced that the RoIRS would be extended beyond the planned 30 June 2022 deadline, until an additional £5m of new funding had been committed. The RoIRS closed to new applications on Wednesday 21 September 2022 when the additional £5m funding had been exhausted. Given the pressing demands for funding across many areas of health and social care no further funding could be assigned to this scheme.Any future funding allocation to a similar scheme to help reduce waiting lists will now need to be considered as part of a broader review of resource investment in Waiting List Initiatives.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of (i) potholes; and (ii) road surface defects, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: Following the reform of local government in 2014, the Department collects and stores data in a manner which mirrors the boundaries of the new district council areas. Road surface defects, including potholes, are managed through the Roads Maintenance Client Management System which records the total number of defects and the number outstanding for each local maintenance office area.This data is regularly published and available for public view through the OpenDataNI portal at https://www.opendatani.gov.uk/dataset/surface-defects and can be filtered by defect type and geographical area.In addition, official statistics on the condition of the road network are also now published through the Northern Ireland Road Network and Condition Statistics. The 2022-23 publication (the first such publication) can be found at https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/northern-ireland-road-network-and-condition-statistics-2022-23

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he will implement any changes following the review of Free School Meals and Uniform Grant.
answer_text: My Department is continuing to progress a review of the eligibility criteria for free school meals and uniform grants.  I will be considering the next steps for this review in the time ahead.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on actions taken by his Department following the Meenbog landslide in November 2020.
answer_text: My officials in NIEA have been working closely with the Loughs Agency which led the criminal investigation of this incident and the subsequent remediation project.  This resulted in a successful prosecution on 7 June 2022 in Letterkenny District Court.DAERA provided advice and funding (£150,000) to the Loughs Agency to enable the necessary monitoring and instream and riparian works on the Mourne Beg and Derg Rivers to be carried out, with the measures targeted to ensure that the river is at optimum quality condition to improve the chances of successful spawning and survival salmonids.  The Loughs Agency continues to monitor the health of the Mourne Beg River, with further surveys scheduled to take place in 2024.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the roll-out of Project Stratum in West Tyrone.
answer_text: A total of 9,989 premises in the West Tyrone Constituency will benefit from funding allocated under Project Stratum. To date, 9,552 premises have been addressed, and now have access to a gigabit-capable broadband service. The remaining 437 will be addressed by June 2025. Further details will be provided at https://hyperfastni.com/.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister what support their Department is giving to the Victims Payment Scheme in relation to the speed of the process and communications with survivors.
answer_text: Almost £38 million has been paid to victims since the Victims’ Payments Scheme opened for applications on 31 August 2021.		We are aware of concerns around the length of time it is taking for applications to reach determination stage.  It is important to acknowledge that each application to the scheme is unique and with its own complexities, and many applications include multiple relevant incidents.	Our department has provided £5 million of funding to support victims’ and survivors’ representative groups to facilitate recruitment of welfare officers and admin support staff who can assist applicants with the application process.  Additional funding has also been made available to provide health and wellbeing support. 		We are also supporting the retrieval of evidence on behalf of applicants and the Victims’ Payments Board.   		Our officials also continue to engage regularly with the Department of Justice and the Victims’ Payments Board to consider ways to help enhance the delivery of the scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when will he make a statement regarding the Planning Appeals Commission report on the A5 Western Transport Corridor Scheme, received on 31 October 2023.
answer_text: The findings presented in the Planning Appeals Commission’s (PAC) advisory report on 31 October 2023 are being considered by officials in consultation with key stakeholders.Once I have reviewed all relevant information in relation to this project, I will be able to make a formal announcement in respect of the scheme.  I am committed to delivering the A5 Western Transport Corridor Scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education why his Department and the Education Authority do not keep central records to detail the number of reported incidents of racism, sectarianism and homophobia in schools.
answer_text: The Addressing Bullying in Schools Northern Ireland Act 2016, which came into effect in 2021, requires schools to record incidents or alleged incidents of bullying type behaviour, their motivation and their outcome.  Each school’s Board of Governors has responsibility to oversee the implementation of the Act in their school.   The legislation does not include the requirement for schools to share the data that they record with the Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the total length, in miles, of (i) rural roads in West Tyrone; and (ii) roads that will be resurfaced in West Tyrone in the 2024/25 financial year.
answer_text: Following the reform of local government in 2014, the Department collects and stores data in a manner which mirrors the boundaries of the new district council areas. The West Tyrone electoral constituency falls within part of the Fermanagh & Omagh District Council and Derry City & Strabane District Council areas.All data relating to road lengths and works carried out are recorded by the Department in kilometres. Below is a table detailing all the road lengths adopted and maintained by the Department in each of the council areas. 2023KmLocal Government District / DfI RoadsMotorwayA Roads (DualA Roads (SingleB RoadsC RoadsUnclassifiedAll road typesdivision[note 4]Carriageway)Carriageway)    Derry City and Strabane0.017.491.3257.6452.01,521.32,339.6Fermanagh and Omagh0.00.6319.3454.1878.62,341.93,994.5Sub Total0.017.9410.6711.71330.73,863.26,334.1The programme of work planned for 2024/25 across West Tyrone will be published in the DfI Roads Annual Report to the relevant Councils and will be produced once budgets for that year are finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the (i) timeline; and (ii) budget to complete the Strule Shared Education Campus.
answer_text: My officials are finalising work on the Full Business Case for the Programme and a potential contractor is in place. On the current programme timetable, subject to all necessary approvals being in place, contract award is, therefore, planned for July 2024.  Assuming successful appointment of a main works contractor and following the pre-construction design period, construction would then begin on site in late 2024. Without ring-fenced additional investment from the Executive, however, this innovative project, which will transform education provision for all the children of Omagh, may not be able to proceed.  I will, therefore, be bringing proposals in regard to funding of the Campus to the Executive in the near future. The estimated total costs of the campus (including costs to date) are £374 million.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on his Department’s consideration of the proposals in the Independent Review of Education.
answer_text: The Department is currently considering the Report and carrying out internal consultation to inform a way forward. This process includes discussion with key education stakeholders including other relevant Departments and Arm’s-Length Bodies.  Following this consultation, the Department will produce a paper for my consideration setting out advice on the various recommendations in the report.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the future of Strabane fire station.
answer_text: Strabane Fire Station is an important part of Northern Ireland Fire & Rescue Services’ (NIFRS) Service Delivery Model and is currently crewed by 22 On-Call Firefighters. These Firefighters provide an emergency response 24 hours per day, 365 days per year to the public of Northern Ireland (NI) as well and undertaking vital community fire safety activities.NIFRS is currently carrying out an exercise to measure and update the risks from fire and other emergencies within Northern Ireland, through the development and implementation of Community Risk Management Plan (CRMP). From 2023, this plan will identify risks across the whole of NI and will inform and underpin any future Service Delivery Model. Any proposed changes will be subject to a full public consultation exercise.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the level of vacant (i) doctor; and (ii) nursing posts, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust area.
To ask the Minister for Communities whether she will be increasing the Rates Support Grant to local councils.
answer_text: I will consider the funding for councils, including Rates Support Grant, in the context of my Department’s overall budget priorities, if any further funding becomes available for the current financial year.My Department’s budget for 2024/25 has not been agreed yet by the Executive and I am therefore unable to confirm any funding at this stage for councils for the next financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether she has considered allocating additional funding to Councils in order for them to roll out a cost of living payment.
answer_text: The AQW was asked of Minister Hargey, the then Minister for Communities, for answer on 9 November 2022, during a period when Ministers were not in post. At that time, Minister Hargey was not in a position to allocate any additional funding to Councils. My Department’s budget for 2024/25 has not been agreed yet by the Executive and I am therefore unable to confirm any funding at this stage for councils for the next financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether her Department is aware of or has concerns around the disposal of the former Castlederg police station.
answer_text: The current legislation, which allows me to remain as Minister, will expire on 28 October 2022 due to the continued absence of a functioning Executive. Unfortunately, my officials will be unable to provide the information sought by the Member before this date to enable me to respond to this AQW.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has assessed the need for additional on-street parking in Newtownstewart.
answer_text: Unfortunately my officials were unable to gather together the information sought by the member in the short amount of time I had remaining in office.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline his Department's relationship with the (i) roads; and (ii) public convenience facilities, at Moorlough Lake.
answer_text: My Department leases the fishing rights for Moorlough from the owners, Abercorn Estates and does not own any land at the site. The Department provides angling infrastructure, such as fishing stands, some of which are suitable use by for disabled anglers.With regard to the laneway around Moorlough, my Department carries out maintenance to ensure DAERA permit holders have access to the angling stands.The toilets were originally installed as part of a water recreation scheme at the site carried out by the then Rivers Agency (DARD). Responsibility for many of these recreation sites was transferred over to local councils many years ago, however it is unclear what management arrangements were put in place for the Moorlough site. The Department does not maintain these facilities and there is no lease in place for the land the toilet block stands on. Prior to the Covid 19 Pandemic, Derry City and Strabane District Council opened and closed the toilets and provided supplies and cleaning services. I understand that there are no plans to reopen the toilet facilities.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for a timeline for the delivery of a new youth centre in Strabane.
answer_text: Following the Department’s approval of the Business Case for the proposed Youth Centre in Strabane, the Education Authority (EA) began negotiations with the owner of the proposed site.However, due to the severely constrained Capital Budget position, the EA is unable to provide a timeline for the delivery of this scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether Derry City and Strabane District Council has contacted his Department regarding the provision of additional off-street parking in Newtownstewart.
answer_text: Unfortunately my officials were unable to gather together the information sought by the member in the short amount of time I had remaining in office.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the footpaths at Orr Park in Newtownstewart will be resurfaced.
answer_text: As you are aware, the legal challenge relating to the award of term contracts for asphalt resurfacing has delayed the current programme of works in the Derry and Strabane areas. Resurfacing of the footways and carriageway in the cul-de-sac at 9 to 22 Orr Park is included within my Department’s programme for the current financial year. However, the cost of resurfacing works have increased significantly during the past twelve months and as such the programme is under review.In the interim my officials will continue to inspect this development and address any defects that meet the current threshold for repair.Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Western Division at dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail all of the cost of living hardship payments to be made available to people in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My officials have worked collaboratively across departments to provide information on all support available in relation to the cost of living and cost of living hardship payments. This information is available on NI Direct’s Cost of Living webpage: https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/campaigns/cost-living. My Department will continue to do all it can to support those experiencing hardship.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of GP practices that have written to (i) the Department; and (ii) Health and Social Care Trusts, to express concern about delivering current GP contracts.
answer_text: Unfortunately due to there being no Minister in post after midnight on the 27th of October, my Department will be unable to answer the question.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the expected completion date of the A6 road upgrades.
answer_text: My officials are working closely with the contractor to have this strategically important project completed as quickly as possible. Current market volatility and supply chain restrictions continue to impact on project delivery but it expected that the road will open within the next few months.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the introduction of additional speed control measures (i) at Bellspark Road at the Glebe; and (ii) on both the Fyfin and Melmount Roads at Victoria Bridge.
answer_text: While I can confirm that my Department currently does not have any plans to introduce additional speed control measures on Bellspark Road at the Glebe, I have instructed local officials to review the current measures to determine if they remain effective.Traffic calming measures in the form of speed cushions have been in place for a number of years on both Fyfin and Melmount Road in Victoria Bridge. The cushions on the Melmount Road have recently been renewed and similar action is being considered for those on the Fyfin Road. Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) to detail the proportion of households in Northern Ireland that would experience income decline as a result of a British government's decision to link benefit uprating to earnings growth rather than inflation; and (ii) for her assessment of the average annual income loss to households in Northern Ireland if this policy is implemented in 2023-24.
answer_text: The information requested at household level is not available for all benefits. I have written to Chloe Smith MP, the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, strongly urging her to up-rate benefits in line with inflation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has assessed the effectiveness of the recently introduced HGV restrictions in Clady village.
answer_text: I can confirm that arrangements are in place to carry out a formal assessment on the effectiveness of the scheme and that a traffic count, including vehicle classification, will be carried out in the coming weeks.Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Western Division at dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer funding.
answer_text: The Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for football is an important flagship Executive commitment and I recognise the valuable contribution it can deliver for football and wider society. I recognise and share the deep frustration of many that this commitment has not yet been delivered. I have asked my officials to present me with proposals and options for consideration as a matter of priority.Once final proposals are confirmed and approvals are secured, I will be able to provide a timeframe for the process to apply for funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether there have been any confirmed cases of defective Mica blocks used in properties in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I can advise that my department was made aware of 6 properties in the Foyle constituency that are owned by Apex Housing Association affected by Mica mineral in 2018. The Housing Association undertook the necessary repairs at these properties.The Housing Executive are not aware of any issues in their housing stock.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what assessment has been made of the budgetary pressures facing Councils, given the increased energy and fuel costs.
answer_text: The funding available for the Department is set within the context of a constrained financial position for 2024/25, and the overall Departmental budget position will be considered against competing priorities. Councils will have considered their funding requirements which includes energy and fuel costs as part of their own budget setting for 2024/25.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on a planned Park and Ride facility in Strabane.
answer_text: In 2019 my Department commissioned a study into the provision of new Park and Ride sites in Western Division including in the Strabane area. The preferred location for Strabane has been identified in proximity to the existing Bus Station.  Further work is required to identify suitable land in this area that might accommodate a Park and Ride facility.  I can confirm that this work remains on my Department’s list of potential future Park and Ride projects.Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Western Division at dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his Department's policy on the positioning of new salt boxes in residential areas.
answer_text: On roads not included on the salted network salt bins (boxes) or grit piles may be provided for use by the public, on a self-help basis. Provision of new salt boxes is not automatic and when considering requests for the provision of a salt bin, my officials carry out an evaluation using a criteria based system which takes into account various factors including topography, traffic volume, road gradient/geometry, alternative routes as well as other factors such as residential usage and community facilities including schools, hospitals and care homes for the elderly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the current financial pressures facing the education budget.
answer_text: The Department’s 2023-24 opening Resource budget was £66.4m or 2.5% less than the budget set by the Secretary of State in 2022-23.Faced with this extremely challenging financial position, the Department took significant decisions to reduce funding allocations, nevertheless it will not be possible to fully control expenditure in areas with a statutory entitlement to provision and rising need such as SEN, school budgets, transport, and Free school Meals.  The Education sector continues to face extensive unavoidable cost pressures and rising service demands associated with delivering statutory and policy obligations and currently estimates a resource funding gap of c£150 million and a capital shortfall of c£43 million in 2023-24. This assessment excludes any provision in respect of outstanding non-contractual teaching pay awards. Education will require more funding during the current and coming budget periods if we are to avoid a significant and detrimental impact on our children and young people.  Education is key to the future economic stability of our society, and we cannot afford to continue to neglect it through years of financial underfunding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail what actions he will take to support staff in our hospitals who are facing crisis conditions.
answer_text: As employers, Health and Social Care Trusts have primary responsibility for supporting staff in our hospitals who are facing crisis conditions.The comprehensive Health and Social Care Workforce Strategy 2026: Delivering for Our People, was published by my Department in May 2018, following detailed engagement with colleagues from across the health and social care system, with the aim that ‘by 2026, we meet our workforce needs and the needs of our workforce’.  The Strategy’s second action plan, which was published on 15th June 2022 and covers the period 2022/23 to 2024/25, contains a range of actions aimed at supporting our staff to remain physically and mentally well at work.  These include an action to develop a HSC staff health and wellbeing framework that will support employers in planning and implementing effective processes and resources for improving staff health, wellbeing and safety at work and an action to commission and establish sustainable occupational health services.  An occupational health workforce review is currently in progress with view to the creation and implementation of a multidisciplinary occupational health workforce plan across the HSC that addresses the impact and learning from the pandemic.  These projects aim to enhance existing occupational health services and established health and wellbeing initiatives provided by HSC employers which include confidential psychological support helplines, reading resources, well-being webinars and online resources on a broad range of subjects that support staff health and wellbeing.A Regional HSC Staff Wellbeing Resource website has also been developed hosting a library of easily accessible resources to complement those provided by individual employers.   HSC staff can search this website for publications, websites links and videos relating to support available on a range of subjects including mental health, grief and bereavement, self-care, dealing with stress and anxiety, domestic abuse, children and young people and families.Ultimately the best way we can support our staff is to grow our workforce to the optimum levels required to deliver treatment and care to everyone in Northern Ireland.  Without sustained long term investment in workforce growth and development, the objectives of the Workforce Strategy will not be achieved.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the capital works planned for schools in West Tyrone.
answer_text: There are currently 3 Major Capital projects, 1 Fresh Start project and 2 School Enhancement projects planned in the West Tyrone constituency.  An update on the Strule project will be provided in the response to AQW 4449/22-27.St Catherine’s Primary School was announced under the Major Capital Works Programme by the former Minister of Education in June 2020. An Integrated Consultant Team has completed a technical feasibility report which details potential options and costs to provide suitable accommodation and outside play space for the school.  This report is currently being reviewed by the Department’s technical advisers in the Education Advisory Branch.  When the review is completed a Business Case will be completed which will identify the preferred option to be taken forward. Gaelscoil Ui Dhochartaigh was announced under the Major Capital Works Programme by the former Minister of Education in January 2013. Business case approval in the sum of £4.7m is in place for the project, with the preferred option being identified as a new build school on Strahans Road, Strabane. The project is in the final stages of building contractor procurement. Dean Maguirc College was announced under the Major Capital Works Programme that I announced in March 2022.  The Education Authority is in the process of procuring an Integrated Consultancy Team (ICT) for the major capital works. It is anticipated that the ICT contract will be awarded in spring 2023. Following appointment, the ICT will undertake a technical feasibility study, which will inform the business case. Drumragh Integrated College, a Fresh Start Project, involves the construction of single storey extension to the existing building, resurfacing of a synthetic pitch and works to the existing car park to improve health and safety on the site. The resurfacing of the synthetic pitch was completed Spring 2022 with the ICT commencing the Developed Design Phase for the extension in September 2022.  It is currently estimated that construction will commence on site in Autumn/Winter 2023. The overall Business Case is for £6.7MSt Mary’s Primary School, Cloughcor, was announced under the School Enhancement Programme for a new extension and some refurbishment of existing building with a Business Case of £3m. The Design Team was appointed in December 2019 and is currently progressing Stage 3 (Developed Design).Strabane Primary School was announced under the School Enhancement Programme for a new extension, reconfiguration of existing accommodation and installation of a new lift with a Business Case of £4m.  A Design Team was appointed in June 2021 and their Technical Feasibility Report is under review by the Education Authority.Numerous other Minor Works projects funded from the Department’s capital budget are also progressing in the West Tyrone constituency, however they are too numerous to list individually.  Capital Projects are complex by their very nature and a variety of factors can contribute to delays which

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the flood alleviation schemes planned for West Tyrone.
answer_text: As you are aware my Department has carried out a number of flood studies to determine if a viable flood alleviation scheme could be taken forward to improve the level of flood protection in Omagh. Unfortunately none of the proposals to date were found to be feasible or economically viable. However, my Department has commissioned a review of the most recent flood study for Omagh Town, which was completed in 2016. The new study, which will take account of climate change commenced in January 2022 and is expected to take 12-18 months to complete.You will also be aware my Department has completed studies to assess the flood risk to properties in both Newtownstewart and Fintona.  The studies for both locations have concluded that none of the potential options to alleviate flooding are economically viable. However, my Department continues to work with multi-agency partners, including Local Government, to provide support to the local community in both Fintona and Newtownstewart through the work of the Regional Community Resilience Group (RCRG). The RCRG works with communities at risk of flooding to help them prepare for and respond to weather related emergencies and have provided access to community sandbag containers in both these areas. My Department has recently received final drafts of feasibility studies for Sion Mills, Elm Wood Green, Castlederg and the ‘Dry Arch’ located in the Lower Market Street, area of Omagh.  Remedial options have been identified which help to alleviate flooding in Sion Mills, however the cost viability is just above parity at present.  Viability will be re-assessed as options are developed in further detail. A cost viable solution has been identified for Elm Wood Green, Castlederg which will help to alleviate flooding.  The study for the ‘Dry Arch’ concluded that whilst there was no viable engineering option to alleviate flooding, it did recommend a ‘Reactive Flood Plan’ option where a pump would be installed in the Dry Arch to effectively draw down flood levels during flooding and reduce the impact on properties. My officials have since met with affected business owners and elected representatives and agreed to construct an adequate sump/man hole within the Dry Arch to further mitigate the risk in times of flooding. My Department has also commissioned a flood study to determine if the existing Strabane flood defences continue to provide the current level of flood protection.  This new study will use the most modern modelling methodology, data and will account for climate change.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether she has plans to increase the provision of Free School Meals for pupils, given the cost of living crisis.
answer_text: The Department is currently undertaking a review of Free School Meals and Uniform Grant eligibility criteria.  The review has considered a wide range of options that include the provision of a universal free school meals to some/all year groups as well as raising the income thresholds that apply to the eligibility criteria for applicants in receipt of Universal Credit or Working/Child Tax Credit. It is also actively considering the option for guidance on the costs of school uniforms to be on a statutory basis. My Department continues to work to develop a costed options paper and it is intended that a public consultation exercise will take place later in the year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on all capital works planned for GP practices in West Tyrone.
answer_text: GP Practices in West Tyrone are delivered from both Trust and GP owned facilities.The Western Trust has received approval to carry out works to develop a shell space within the Omagh Hospital & Primary Care Centre to support the roll out of the Primary Care Multi-Disciplinary Team Transformation Project.  This work is currently at design stage. The Trust has also carried out works to the Carrickmore Health Centre to provide additional space for the GP practice within the Health Centre in order to meet the urgent and immediate needs of the practice. It is recognised that further works to the Health Centre are required to meet the growing requirements of the practice in delivering services to its patients. Once funding is made available to my Department, the Trust will be invited to submit an Outline Business Case to consider development options for Carrickmore Health Centre.The Western Health and Social Care Trust continue to engage with my Department to develop an Outline Business Case for the potential development of a Health & Social Care Hub in Strabane Health Centre, as part of the City Deal Project.  With regards to GP owned premises, my Department continues to engage with GP practices across the West Tyrone area to assess the need for capital investment in their premises. GP practices are invited to apply to for a GP Improvement capital grant to assist with premises developments. Dunamanagh GP practice has availed of a capital grant to extend its premises to facilitate GP training and the roll out of multi-disciplinary teams whilst refurbishment of Castlederg Group Surgery practice was also carried out to provide space for the introduction of multi-disciplinary teams.Newtownstewart Medical Practice has received approval in principle to extend to its premises to increase capacity for both General Medical Services and the facilitation of Multi-disciplinary teams.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the (i) timeline; and (ii) budget to complete the Strule Shared Education Campus.
answer_text: The previous Main Works procurement competition has been closed and the Department formally commenced a fresh Main Works procurement competition with the release of the Invitation to Tender on 13 December 2021.On the current programme timetable, subject to all necessary approvals being in place, contract award is planned for spring 2023.  Assuming successful appointment of a main works contractor and following the pre-construction design period, construction is due to begin on site autumn 2023 and is currently planned to complete in 2026, with campus opening planned for September 2026.The estimated budget required to complete the Campus is £183m.  This includes the costs of construction, including a contingency for construction price inflation, risk and optimism bias; staffing the Programme; and other running costs, including support being provided to the schools for shared curricular development and implementation.  Costs will continue to be kept under review.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the social housing waiting list in West Tyrone, broken down for each of the last three years.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows.“Please see table below with waiting list figures for the West Tyrone Parliamentary Constituency as at end December for each of the last 3 years”.Dec-23Dec-22Dec-21Applicants1,9391,8701,871”

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether her Department will top up the Hardship Fund set up by Derry City and Strabane District Council.
answer_text: The cost-of-living crisis is having major impact, particularly on those who are already struggling financially. I am deeply frustrated that the continued absence of a functioning Executive and agreed Executive budget prevents additional measures being introduced.	My Department will do all it can to support those experiencing hardship, and all options are being explored to provide both immediate and medium to longer term interventions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the total length, in miles, of rural roads in West Tyrone; and (ii) the length, in miles, of roads that will be resurfaced in West Tyrone in the 2022/23 financial year.
answer_text: There are 1886.71 miles of rural roads in the West Tyrone constituency. My Department has programmed to resurface 25.19 miles of the public road network in the West Tyrone constituency during the 2022/23 financial year. However this programme is under review due to an unprecedented increase in costs due primarily to the volatility of oil prices.Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Western Division at dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a timeline for the completion of the A5 Western Transport Corridor Scheme.
answer_text: I am committed to doing all that I can during my time as Minister for Infrastructure to advance this vitally important flagship project.Discussions are ongoing with the PAC on the timing of a reconvened public inquiry. Receipt of the PAC’s final report should then allow a new formal Ministerial decision to be taken on whether to proceed with the scheme.Subject to the successful completion of all the necessary statutory processes and environmental assessments, the construction phase could then commence by early 2024.The programme for scheme delivery in recent years has alluded to full scheme completion by 2028 and although some slippage has occurred, this timeframe remains achievable.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on a pay rise for teachers and school support staff.
answer_text: Teachers’ PayThe Department of Education (DE) is not the employer of teachers. Teachers’ terms and conditions, including pay, are negotiated by the Teachers’ Negotiating Committee (TNC).  Management Side of the TNC includes representatives from the employers, sectoral interests and DE.  Teachers’ Side of the TNC includes representatives from the Northern Ireland Teachers’ Council (NITC) comprising the five recognised teachers’ unions. My Department is not responsible for the management of the TNC although it is represented on it.Engagement has been ongoing over the last number of months between Management Side and the NITC to explore options for a teachers’ pay settlement for 2021-22.  Following receipt of the NITC pay claim for 2022-23, these discussions are now considering the options for a multi-year pay deal, which will be dependent on the Education budget and Public Sector Pay Policy for 2022/23.   School Support StaffThe vast majority of non-teaching staff in Northern Ireland are employed on National Joint Council (NJC) terms and conditions. NJC pay awards are negotiated nationally and Department of Education officials are not involved in these negotiations.  However, I am aware that the National Employers in England made a pay offer to the unions representing the main NJC workforce on 25 July 2022.  Details of the offer are as follows:with effect from 1 April 2022, an increase of £1,925 on all NJC pay points;with effect from 1 April 2022, an increase of 4.04 per cent on all allowances;with effect from 1 April 2023, an increase of one day annual leave entitlement; andwith effect from 1 April 2023, the deletion of pay point 1 from the NJC pay spine.The proposed increase of £1,925 to all pay points equates to a percentage increase of between 4.04% and 10.5%, depending on the relevant pay point of the individual. The offer from National Employers is currently being considered by Trade Unions, with results of ballots expected by the end of October.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his plans to increase road safety along the current A5 road.
answer_text: My Department is working to improve safety on all of our roads, including the A5.To this end I would like to reaffirm my commitment to the delivery of the A5 Western Transport Corridor project which will greatly improve the safety for all road users and benefit many communities living close to the existing A5 route. The removal of the strategic traffic from the existing road will leave it much safer for all road users and improve the quality of life for those who reside in the vicinity of this busy road.I recognise that road safety has long been an issue on the existing A5 and I remain committed to carrying out road improvements on this strategic route until such time that the proposed A5 dual carriageway is constructed. In recent years there have been a number of schemes completed on the A5 including resurfacing and realignment works as well as improvements for pedestrians at Ballymacilroy.  Resurfacing, realignment of the carriageway and construction of laybys was also completed at Milltown close to Victoria Bridge. I am pleased to confirm that there are a number of proposed road improvements on the A5 included within the Local Transport and Safety Measures Programme for 2022-2023.  Works to provide street lighting at Garvaghy commenced on Monday 3 October 2022. This will complement new road markings and signage which were recently installed at this location.  There are also proposals to upgrade the road signage on the A5 from Aughnacloy to Ballygawley.  This follows the recent upgrade of road markings and signage along the A5 between Ballygawley to Omagh.Other proposed works include a Junction Improvement Scheme on the A5 Great Northern Road, Omagh at Homebase Junction and three resurfacing schemes which will improve the skid resistance on the existing road.My officials will continue to work closely with the PSNI to identify any further measures that can be taken to improve safety on the existing A5 road.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on her Department's interest in acquiring the site of the former Castlederg police station.
answer_text: I refer you to my response to your previous question (AQW 1396/22-27).

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the future of Castlederg Ambulance Station.
answer_text: NIAS is considering its strategic service plan for Castlederg and has engaged with local MLAs to discuss service provision.  There are no plans to reduce the level of ambulance coverage in this area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his Department's policy on replacing back street lighting in residential areas.
answer_text: When street lighting is deemed to have reached the end of its life and needs to be replaced, the area is assessed and new lighting is provided only in those areas that meet my Department’s policy for the provision of lighting.In urban residential areas, street lighting is provided on adopted pathways which provide access to the front entrances of properties, or where they are identified as through routes with no reasonable alternative lit route. However, where a pathway is not adopted, or is identified as only providing access to the rear of properties, any existing lighting is removed and new lighting is not provided.This policy ensures that my Department’s finite resources are directed at those areas where maximum benefit to the public can be derived, particularly where road or infrastructure safety is concerned, whilst also seeking to reduce future maintenance and energy costs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the installation of bollards on the A5 at Victoria Bridge.
answer_text: My officials have recently confirmed that the bridge on the A5 at Victoria Bridge is suitable for the installation of bollards and a works instruction has issued with this work expected to be carried out shortly.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the plans he has to improve road safety on the A5 road.
answer_text: My Department is working to improve safety on all of our roads, including the A5.To this end I would like to reaffirm my commitment to the delivery of the A5 Western Transport Corridor project which will greatly improve the safety for all road users and benefit many communities living close to the existing A5 route. The removal of the strategic traffic from the existing road will leave it much safer for all road users and improve the quality of life for those who reside in the vicinity of this busy road.I recognise that road safety has long been an issue on the existing A5 and I remain committed to carrying out road improvements on this strategic route until such time that the proposed A5 dual carriageway is constructed.
