question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how she intends to support community centres, given the increase in their running costs.
answer_text: The cost-of-living crisis is having major impact, particularly on those who are already struggling financially. I am deeply frustrated that the continued absence of a functioning Executive and agreed Executive budget prevents additional measures being introduced.	My Department will do all it can to support those experiencing hardship, and all options are being explored to provide both immediate and medium to longer term interventions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the All Island Strategic Rail Review.
answer_text: As you will be aware, the Public Consultation phase of the All Island Strategic Rail Review closed on 21st January 2022. Work on the review has been progressing and I recently met with Minister Ryan and ARUP in advance of the Review’s anticipated completion in the final quarter of 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department have powers to mitigate increases in the mortgage interest rate.
answer_text: It is completely unacceptable that the announcements the Chancellor made in his mini-budget will result in increased mortgage costs for many thousands of homeowners in the midst of a cost of living crisis. These are reserved matters, and given the scale of the increased costs many will now face, the powers and responsibility for addressing this situation rest with the British Government.  Therefore I, alongside my Welsh and Scottish counterparts, jointly wrote to the Chancellor on 30th September 2022 highlighting our concerns around the economic and financial turmoil his announcements have caused and calling for further targeted support to help those who need it.I will continue to press on the British Government that a completely different approach must be adopted to support people with the higher costs they face as a direct result of their actions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) whether his Department is aware of concerns around Tuberculosis in the Trillick area of West Tyrone; and (ii) to outline what plans he has to address the issue.
answer_text: My officials have made me aware of increased instances of bovine Tuberculosis (bTB) in the Trillick area of County Tyrone.  Staff at the Omagh DAERA Direct office are using all the tools at their disposal to reduce the bTB levels in the locality.  For each bTB breakdown, officials continue to conduct field investigations to establish the source(s) of infection, forward and backward trace animals and identify contiguous herds at risk of infection.  Officials will continue the use of severe and, where applicable, super severe interpretation of the tuberculin skin test and will continue to strongly encourage all eligible herds to partake in interferon gamma (IFNg) testing.  The IFNg test assists in identifying positive animals at an earlier stage and their removal helps prevent further spread of infection.  Local veterinary officials will also continue to educate and encourage farmers on ways to further improve their trading and biosecurity practices to reduce the risk of the herd becoming infected with bTB. I am acutely aware of the challenges and significant stress that bTB breakdowns bring to farmers and their families.  Since taking up office as Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, I have made bTB eradication a key priority and, in March this year, I launched a new long-term Strategy to reduce and ultimately eradicate this disease from Northern Ireland.  The Strategy contains 21 actions across 6 thematic areas that are intended to address all the factors that contribute to the spread and endurance of this disease.  It is designed to reduce the impact of bTB in the short term, while laying the foundations for its eventual eradication.  My officials are working closely with the TB Eradication Partnership and our stakeholder partners to implement the Strategy’s actions.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of (i) potholes; and (ii) road surface defects, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: My Department does not maintain statistics solely in relation to potholes but rather focuses on the total number of surface defects which, as well as potholes, also includes defects such as cracking and rutting. In addition, my Department does not maintain records by constituency but rather maintains this information on a Council or Roads Section Office area basis. The following table provides details by council area of the number of carriageway, hard shoulder and layby surface defects (which includes potholes, cracking, depressions etc.) that had been recorded and listed for repair since 1 April 2022 and, as of 21 September 2022, do not yet have a completion date recorded against them:CouncilOutstanding Repairs 1 Apr 2022 - 20 Sep 2022Antrim and Newtownabbey1098Ards and North Down263Armagh City, Banbridge and Craigavon1309Belfast1157Causeway Coast and Glens305Derry City and Strabane3199Fermanagh and Omagh1092Lisburn and Castlereagh418Mid and East Antrim795Mid Ulster679Newry, Mourne and Down1643It is important to note that there can be a time lag between when a repair is carried out and when the associated records are updated on the Department’s maintenance management system. As a result, the figure supplied for the number of outstanding defects may reduce as records are updated.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the provision of a road safety barrier on the Curley Hill, Strabane.
answer_text: Design work for a vehicle restraint system at Curleyhill Road adjacent to the Altiskane development, Strabane is currently being progressed.  Upon completion of the design work the intention is to proceed with construction of the restraint system later this financial year subject to the availability of contractor resource.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for an update on the current Ballycolman flood scheme; and (ii) whether he plans on bringing forward a further scheme.
answer_text: I am pleased to be able to confirm that work to implement the short-term flooding alleviation scheme is nearing completion.With regard to the longer-term option, I have been advised by NI Water that its contractors are currently gathering the extensive site information required for the proposed tunnelling of the gravity sewer. Due to the complex nature of this work, the site investigation stage is taking longer than had originally been anticipated. Preparation works have started and on-site investigations are expected to commence in late 2022. The completion of this work will allow detailed design of the scheme to be finalised.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the A5 Western Transport Corridor project.
answer_text: I am committed to doing all that I can during my time as Minister for Infrastructure to advance this vitally important flagship project.A new Environmental Statement Addendum and associated documentation, including a new draft Vesting Order, were published on 16 March 2022, commencing a consultation process that concluded on 6 May. All representations have been collated and discussions are ongoing with the PAC on the timing of a reconvened public inquiry. Receipt of the PAC’s final report should then allow a new formal Ministerial decision to be taken on whether to proceed with the scheme.Subject to the successful completion of all the necessary statutory processes and environmental assessments, the construction phase could then commence.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on road safety improvements at the Killeter and Cavan Road junction in Castlederg.
answer_text: I can confirm that the red gateway surfacing on the Killeter Road approach to the Killeter Road/Cavan Road junction was renewed in September.   I am also pleased to be able to confirm that an additional pair of traffic calming road cushions are programmed for installation on the town side of the Killeter Road/Cavan Road junction in October of this year.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has considered a fund to bring historic unadopted roads up to standard across the north.
answer_text: I refer the member to my previous reply to AQW 488/22-27. The Roads (Northern Ireland) Order 1993, confers legal authority on my Department to carry out works on those public roads that it maintains. This Order does not provide any legal authority to permit my Department to undertake resurfacing works on unadopted or private roads.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the roads that will be resurfaced in West Tyrone as part of the awarding of road resurfacing contracts, announced on 30th June 2022.
answer_text: Details of the resurfacing programme for West Tyrone, which includes parts of the Fermanagh & Omagh and Derry City & Strabane Council areas, will be included in the Department’s Council Reports which are currently being finalised following recent confirmation of the Division’s 2022/23 contingency funding allocation. The Reports will, in due course be presented to the Councils, published on the Department’s website, and placed in the Assembly library.Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Western Division at dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on funding for the Sub-regional Stadia programme.
answer_text: The challenges created by not having a functioning Executive mean I cannot bring new proposals for endorsement and agreement as is required. I met with representatives from IFA and NIFL, and have agreed to work with them, and other stakeholders, to progress.I remain committed to moving this Programme forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a timeline on the resurfacing of the road at Jefferson Court, Strabane.
answer_text: A scheme to resurface the carriageway at Jefferson Court is included within the Department’s two year maintenance programme.  It is currently scheduled to be progressed during the 2023/24 financial year, subject to sufficient funding being made available.  In the intervening period my officials will continue to carry out periodic inspections of this development and action for repair any defects which meet the intervention thresholds defined in our current maintenance policies.Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Western Division at dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the locations of permanent speed indicator devices across Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The majority of Speed Indicator Devices (SIDs) have been installed by local Policing and Community Safety Partnerships (PCSPs) and a list of their locations is not held by my Department. I would suggest that you contact the local PCSPs for this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the proposed new build social homes in West Tyrone.
answer_text: In the West Tyrone parliamentary constituency there are currently 283 social housing units under construction.A further 62 social housing units are programmed to start as part of the Social Housing Development Programme (SHDP) during the period 2022/23 – 2024/25. Programmed schemes can be lost or slip to future programme years for a variety of reasons e.g. relating to delays in acquiring sites and/or failure to secure Planning Approval. Additional schemes can also be added to the SHDP on an ‘in-year’ basis, through the Competitive Design & Build procurement route and/or the purchase of Existing Satisfactory / or Off-the-Shelf properties.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department is planning to increase the budget for mental health and addictions services in West Tyrone.
answer_text: The Mental Health Strategy 2021-31 sets out 35 actions as a roadmap to promote good mental health and improve mental health services.  It includes a commitment (at Action 28) to create a Managed Care Network with experts in dual diagnosis, that will support and build capacity in both mental health and substance use services and which will ensure that these services meet the full needs of those with co-occurring issues. Full implementation of the Mental Health Strategy is dependent on the provision of additional and sustained funding to my Department.  The Funding Plan that was published alongside the Strategy identified an additional funding requirement of £1.2bn over the period of the strategy. In September 2021, I launched Northern Ireland's new 10-year Substance Use Strategy "Preventing Harm & Empowering Recovery: A Strategic Framework to Tackle Substance Use". Underscored by five population-level outcomes, the vision of the Strategy is that people are supported in the prevention and reduction of the harm related to the use and misuse of alcohol and other drugs, and will be empowered to maintain recovery.A health and social care needs assessment is currently being commissioned by the Public Health Agency and the Strategic Planning & Performance Group (SPPG) for the population of the Western Trust (WHSCT) area in relation to substance use issues.  On completion, this assessment will provide a comprehensive understanding of the challenges experienced within the WHSCT area relating to substance use and will include the scoping of existing services and resources in order to support the identification of gaps in service provision.  This assessment will inform commissioning decisions and will identify potential opportunities for additional investment with regards to any areas needing service development and/or improvement.In addition, the Public Health Agency and SPPG are currently developing a new comprehensive, whole-system strategic plan for substance use services and support, which will address demographic trends in increasing demand and complexity, current and future resource, current gaps in service provision, service pressures, workforce issues and equity of access to support across Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department plans to resurface any of the roads in Strabane town centre, given the delays in progressing the Public Realm Scheme for Strabane.
answer_text: My officials inform me that Derry City and Strabane District Council still hope to progress the Public Realm Scheme in the foreseeable future. The works involved in delivering the Public Realm Scheme will affect the condition of the existing roads in the town centre. As a result, resurfacing the roads in advance of the proposed scheme would not be an effective use of public funds.Until the Public Realm scheme is constructed the roads in Strabane Town Centre will continue to be maintained in line with the Department’s Road Maintenance Standards for Safety – Limited Service policy.Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Western Division at dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department is responsible for the green areas within the Castletown Court development in Strabane.
answer_text: I can confirm that my Department is not responsible for maintenance of the green areas within the Castletown Court development in Strabane.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the provision of advice services in West Tyrone.
answer_text: My Department and Fermanagh and Omagh District Council make a combined annual investment of around £276,000 in independent, community based advice services, including assistance with benefit appeals and tribunal representation.   In addition, my Department funds dedicated debt advisors, through Advice NI, to deliver a free confidential debt service in this Council area.In the region of 5,300 people benefited from these services in the last financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline the actions his Department is taking to mitigate the impact that the increasing cost of fuel and supplies is having on the farming community.
answer_text: I am fully aware of the pressures that Northern Ireland producers are facing in relation to unprecedented rises in input costs of energy, feed and fertiliser which simply cannot be absorbed by farm businesses. I note that markets for agricultural products are beginning to respond, which is welcome. However, end markets for agricultural produce not yet responding sufficiently to offset rising costs. Farmers are experiencing significant adverse effects on their cash flows and profitability. This is undermining confidence throughout the industry and will inevitably result in a contraction of production due to mounting losses if the situation is not corrected very soon. This correction must come in the form of increased farm gate prices for all sectors or the economic viability of primary production and domestic supplies of agricultural goods cannot be sustained. I have written to retailers to voice my concern regarding farm gate prices for primary food producers.I continue to engage with the UK Government and raised the current issues that our industry is facing at the last Inter-Ministerial Group meeting.  I have also engaged with a wide range of agri-food stakeholders and industry representatives to see what mitigation measures are possible. My Officials have significant ongoing engagement and intelligence gathering from industry stakeholders, DEFRA, other devolved administrations and the Department of Agriculture, Food and Marine (ROI) on a number of cross cutting issues relating to reducing the impacts of volatility in price and supply of key industry inputs.  Officials at The College of Agriculture Food and Rural Enterprise (CAFRE) lead an industry taskforce (banks, producer and supply chain organisations, farming unions and Rural Support) to enhance the communication of advisory messages to the agriculture industry around the cost and availability of inputs and provide the sector with practical help and support.  CAFRE has a dedicated webpage providing advice on managing the rising costs of production and delivered a series of on-farm events.Since the situation that our agri-food industry is facing is a UK-wide problem I have written to the Secretary of State, Rt Hon George Eustice MP, to urgently consider a range of measures, including financial assistance for UK farmers to support them through the crisis.  I met the Rt. Hon George Eustice MP, on 08 June 2022, to reiterate once again my call for a funding package for the agricultural and fisheries sectors as quickly as possible. I have also written to the Finance Minister, Conor Murphy MLA, seeking funding to help address the challenges currently facing the agricultural sector in Northern Ireland. These pressures on the industry are not temporary and we do not want to jeopardise our domestic food supply.I have also attended a Four Nation Fuel summit as I am concerned that the significant rise in fuel cost is jeopardising our ability to provide resilience in food supply. I am also writing to R
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the Derry and Strabane City Deal.
answer_text: The Derry City and Strabane District Council City Deal is working through the business case process in order to reach the stage for signing of a Deal document. City Deals are long term capital investment programmes and there is a significant amount of work to be done in developing business cases between Heads of Terms and agreeing a Deal document. Ongoing engagement continues between the council, delivery partners and the relevant departments.The council is hoping to be in a position to sign the Deal in spring next year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on the financial support scheme for pig farmers announced in March 2022.
answer_text: The Pig Sector Support Scheme opened on Wednesday 18 May 2022 and closed on Thursday 26 May 2022.  To date payments of just over £1.2m have been made to 129 producers with 4 further payments worth £66k to be made in coming days.The Scheme targeted pig farmers impacted by the effects of weakening markets, increased feed costs and getting pigs moved off the farm for slaughter.  Payments were made to those pig producers who incurred price penalties on overweight pigs above normal baseline levels, during the scheme period (1 September 2021 to 28 February 2022).  In addition, producers who were charged £52.50 per head by Cranswick Country Foods to slaughter pigs on Saturday shifts in 2021 are also eligible for support.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the modernisation of Strabane Courthouse.
answer_text: There are currently no specific plans for the modernisation of Strabane Courthouse.	In 2021, the Northern Ireland Courts and Tribunals Service published a Modernisation Vision Statement (https://www.justice-ni.gov.uk/publications/nicts-modernisation-portfolio-vision-statement), including a commitment to taking forward a major capital project to deliver modern, flexible court and tribunal accommodation for the North-West. This project has been initiated to evaluate investments in the NICTS facilities in this region, which includes Strabane.			Work on this project has been curtailed due to the Covid-19 pandemic, and remains at an early stage. As such, it is not possible to make any unequivocal statements regarding investments or initiatives in any specific building.		I hope this is helpful.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of the current milk price and the return that farmers receive.
answer_text: The latest milk price statistics published by my Department on 21 June 2022, indicate that the weighted average farm gate price for milk during the period January to April 2022 was 38.21 pence per litre.  This is 7.98 pence per litre (26%) higher than the price paid for the same period in 2021.  Overall, milk prices during 2022 have been well above historic levels and NI prices are currently 10% above the UK average price (April 2022) reflecting our greater dependence on dairy commodity markets.    Although milk prices in 2022 are higher, input costs have also increased substantially due mainly to price pressures in world markets for feed, fertiliser and fuels.  At this stage, it is estimated that feed, fertiliser and fuel prices have risen by 30%, 200%, and 50% respectively from last year.  These increases in input costs are certainly offsetting to some extent the increased revenue that farmers are receiving due to the higher milk prices.  The overall impact of all these changes remains to be seen.  Full estimates of dairy farm returns for 2022/23 will be set out in the farm income statistics that will be published by my Department in early 2023.  In the meantime I will continue to monitor the situation.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the Campaign for Borders Rail in Scotland and the lessons that can be learned for the future of rail expansion in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I am aware of the Border Railways project connecting Tweedbank and the Borders area with Edinburgh and the rest of the GB National Rail network. This project to reconstruct a 30-mile section of railway line last used in 1969 was completed in 2015 and has demonstrated positive social and economic benefits.I am committed to addressing regional imbalance and I am aware of the benefits, both economically and socially, for our communities of an enhanced rail network. Future development of the rail network here presents a unique opportunity to improve the sustainability of our transport operations. However, as in Scotland, investment decisions need to consider its operational and financial viability, and examine its wider role in assisting the growth of the region.  We also need to consider the most effective use of public money in contributing to the achievement of the emissions targets set in the Climate Change Act. In line with that, my Department is currently developing the new Regional Strategic Transport Network Transport Plan (RSTNTP) which will set out priorities for investment in our road, rail and inter-urban limited stop bus network, including park & ride, through to 2035.This will, in association with Translink, include high level assessments on a variety of potential rail improvements. The RSTNTP will be subject to public consultation and I will be pleased to consider representations on rail issues, including the reinstatement of the former rail routes, as part of this consultation.You will also be aware that work is ongoing through the All Island Strategic Rail Review to take a strategic look at our rail network to determine how best it can deliver for our citizens in the future.  As part of work on the Review, the reopening of Borders Railway line is being used as a case study for the Strategic Rail Review to understand what we can learn from that experience. Whilst this work is ongoing, I can confirm that the review is anticipated to be completed in the autumn.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will assess the current condition of the old Great Northern Railway bed from Derry City to Strabane, with regard to the potential future rail expansion in the North West.
answer_text: With the Climate Act now in place the importance of an accessible rail network comes into new focus and I believe that will include the need for new rail connections and new rail halts to ensure as many people as possible can access a rail network that meets their everyday work and leisure needs as well as driving economic benefits in the area.In that regard, my Department is undertaking work to take a strategic look at the rail network to determine how best it can deliver for our citizens in the future. One element of this work is the All-Island Strategic Rail Review. The Review is being undertaken to allow us to consider our rail network across this island with a view to how it can be improved for everyone. It will examine the potential for rail connections across the island including how our existing connections could be improved and where new rail links would be best placed. It will also examine how we can connect our rail network to areas across the Island of Ireland including links between Derry and Strabane as well as giving consideration to our international gateways through our ports and airports, the potential for transporting freight using the rail network whilst also considering how we can move to a low or zero emission rail network. It is anticipated that the review will be completed in the autumn.The other element of this work is the development of the new Regional Strategic Transport Network Transport Plan (RSTNTP) which will set out priorities for investment in our road, rail and inter-urban limited stop bus network, including park & ride, through to 2035.This will, in association with Translink, include high level assessments on a variety of potential rail improvements. The RSTNTP will be subject to public consultation and I will be pleased to consider representations on rail issues, including the reinstatement of the former Great Northern Railway route between Strabane and Derry, as part of this consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the proposed road safety improvements at the Cavan Road and Killeter Road junction in Castlederg.
answer_text: In addition to the work already carried out which included installation of an additional ‘SLOW’ marking on the Killeter Road, my Department is planning to renew the red gateway surfacing during the summer months when the more favourable weather conditions required to undertake this work are expected.  I am also pleased to be able to confirm that additional traffic calming measures in the form of a road hump will also be provided later this year as part of a wider programme being implemented throughout the Derry City & Strabane Council area.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will be erecting a barrier on the Curlyhill Road that runs adjacent to the Altiskane development, Strabane.
answer_text: I can confirm that the design work for a vehicle restraint system at Curleyhill Road adjacent to the Altiskane development, Strabane is currently being progressed.  Upon completion of the design work it is intended to commence construction of the system later this financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the installation of speed humps in Spamount, Tyrone.
answer_text: I am pleased to confirm that it is my Department’s intention to install traffic calming features in the form of road humps on Drumnabey Road later this financial year. It forms part of a wider package of traffic calming schemes being carried out within the Derry City & Strabane Council area.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the current waiting lists for extensions to Housing Executive homes and the length of time applicants have been waiting.
answer_text: The Housing Executive has advised there are 278 properties awaiting a Major Adaptation with 66 currently on hold for reasons including tenant illness and issues obtaining statutory approvals.  Of the remaining 212 Major Adaptations 66% have been waiting under 1 year, 29% waiting 1-2 years and 5% waiting over 2 years.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline the support available for domiciliary care workers in the private sector who are struggling with increased fuel costs.
answer_text: You will be aware that on 24 November I announced a support package of up to £23m which allowed independent domiciliary care providers to improve recruitment, employee retention and Terms and Conditions for staff.  This investment has already allowed providers to deliver enhanced rates of pay and improve other terms and conditions. Despite a very challenging financial climate I have ensured that sufficient funding was provided to Trusts to ensure this uplift was carried through to the current year and has been made recurrent.  I am acutely aware of the continuing impact that the increase in living costs and fuel prices is having and was pleased to recently be able to announce a mileage rate increase for Agenda for Change (AfC) staff, including Trust employed domiciliary care workers and district nurses.I have tasked officials to continue engaging with the independent sector on ongoing challenges including the travel and other cost of living pressures facing staff. However, given the financial pressures and budgetary uncertainty, the room for manoeuvre in terms of additional funding is limited.Proposals currently out for consultation on the Reform of Adult Social Care will see wider ranging changes to the delivery of social care. However, this reform package will require sustained additional investment and budgetary certainty.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he plans to subsidise fuel costs for domiciliary care workers in the private sector.
answer_text: You will be aware that on 24 November I announced a support package of up to £23m which allowed independent domiciliary care providers to improve recruitment, employee retention and Terms and Conditions for staff.  This investment has already allowed providers to deliver enhanced rates of pay and improve other terms and conditions. Despite a very challenging financial climate I have ensured that sufficient funding was provided to Trusts to ensure this uplift was carried through to the current year and has been made recurrent.  I am acutely aware of the continuing impact that the increase in living costs and fuel prices is having and was pleased to recently be able to announce a mileage rate increase for Agenda for Change (AfC) staff, including Trust employed domiciliary care workers and district nurses.I have tasked officials to continue engaging with the independent sector on ongoing challenges including the travel and other cost of living pressures facing staff. However, given the financial pressures and budgetary uncertainty, the room for manoeuvre in terms of additional funding is limited.Proposals currently out for consultation on the Reform of Adult Social Care will see wider ranging changes to the delivery of social care. However, this reform package will require sustained additional investment and budgetary certainty.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether there are plans for additional capital investment in addiction services in West Tyrone.
answer_text: Last September, I launched Northern Ireland's new 10-year Substance Use Strategy "Preventing Harm & Empowering Recovery: A Strategic Framework to Tackle Substance Use". Underscored by five population-level outcomes, the vision of the new strategy is that people in Northern Ireland are supported in the prevention and reduction of the harm related to the use and misuse of alcohol and other drugs, and will be empowered to maintain recovery.A Health & Social Care needs assessment is currently being commissioned by the Public Health Agency and the Strategic Planning & Performance Group (SPPG) for the population of Western Health & Social Care Trust (WHSCT) area in relation to substance use issues. On completion, this assessment will provide a comprehensive understanding of the challenges experienced within the WHSCT area relating to substance use and will include the scoping of existing services and resources in order to support the identification of gaps in service provision. This assessment will inform commissioning decisions in relation to WHSCT area and will identify potential opportunities for additional investment with regards to any areas needing service development and/or improvement as part of the wider commissioning process.In addition, the PHA and SPPG are currently developing a new comprehensive, whole-system strategic plan for substance use services and support, which will address demographic trends in increasing demand and complexity, current and future resource, current gaps in service provision, service pressures, workforce issues and equity of access to support across the Region.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the planned road safety improvements for the A5 road at Garvaghy.
answer_text: My Department remains committed to improving road safety along the A5 and my officials are working to implement a range of safety measures at Garvaghy.New Gateway signs have recently been erected on the A5 approaches to Garvaghy and these will soon be accompanied by a 10 metre strip of red high friction surfacing and a 50mph roundel painted onto the road.  Red high friction surfacing and white hatching will also be laid on the hatched out areas in the centre of carriageway on the approach to the junctions. These measures will highlight to motorists they are entering a settlement with numerous accesses and should encourage road users to take additional care and adhere to the applicable speed limit. It is expected this work will be undertaken by our contractor in the coming weeks.Work on the detailed design for Street Lighting at Garvaghy is currently ongoing and I am advised that it is anticipated this work will be delivered on the ground before the end of the year.I am also fully committed to the delivery of the A5 Western Transport Corridor project which will greatly improve the safety for all road users and benefit many communities living close to the existing A5 route, like those at Garvaghy. However in the meantime my Department is continuing to consider if there are any further safety measures that may be appropriate at this location, including improved pedestrian facilities.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for an update on the 20mph speed limit scheme outside schools; (ii) whether he will be progressing a third phase of this scheme; and (iii) whether any schools in the Derg electoral area will be included in further phases of the scheme.
answer_text: My Department has invested £2million over the past two financial years which will see the introduction of part-time 20mph speed limits at 215 schools.  The second tranche of part-time 20mph speed limits at schools is being delivered this year. When all of the tranche 2 sites are fully operational and have had sufficient time to bed in, my Department intends to carry out a review of this work to determine if a further tranche is justified. Until this review is carried out I am not in a position to commit to install part-time 20mph speed limits at any more schools.Should you have any further questions on this issue, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk .

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will consider erecting a permanent speed indicator device on the Garvaghy section of the current A5 road.
answer_text: The placement of a permanent Speed Indicator Device (SID) on the A5 at Garvaghy is a matter for Fermanagh and Omagh District Council, specifically through its Policing and Community Safety Partnership (PCSP). Although my Department is consulted on proposals, it is the PCSP and Council which make the final decision on the number of SID’s deployed and their specific locations. Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his Departments policy in erecting permanent speed indicator devices on roads.
answer_text: Although Speed Indicator Devices (SIDs) are not considered to be traffic signs, my Department acknowledges they offer some road safety benefits and is content that groups such as local Policing and Community Safety Partnerships (PCSPs) continue to deploy them.  A legal issue has been identified regarding the attachment of such devices to Departmental property such as street lighting columns, and my Department is currently working with the Department of Justice and the PSNI to establish how best to facilitate the placement of SIDs at locations where they are likely to have a positive effect on driver behaviour as well as road safety.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the spend by each Health and Social Care Trust on private sector health care over the last three years.
answer_text: Expenditure on the independent health care sector in each of the last three years is set out in the table below.Health & Social Care Trust2019/202020/212021/22 £m£m£mBelfast Health & Social Care Trust13.59.622.8Northern Health & Social Care Trust2.61.85Southern Health & Social Care Trust2.24.67.5South Eastern Health & Social Care Trust5.89.614.6Western Health & Social Care Trust0.21.69TOTAL24.327.258.9Table 1HSC Trusts have provided the expenditure on independent and private health providers shown in Table 1. The figures do not include expenditure on the independent and private residential and nursing home sector.Expenditure on 20/21 Quarter 1 cost recovery is not included as the HSCB paid this directly to independent sector providers. This was therefore not a direct cost to Trusts, although the independent sector providers treated Trust patients.The expenditure in Table 1 relates to outpatient, inpatient, daycase and diagnostics activity. Both expenditure and activity reflect the impact, on the wider HSC system, of variations in available capacity, funding resources, and the COVID pandemic from 2019/20 to 2021/22.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on flood alleviation plans for the Dry Arch in Omagh.
answer_text: As you will be aware my Department has commissioned its partner consultant to carry out a feasibility study for the Dry Arch located in the Lower Market Street, Campsie area of Omagh. This study is to determine if a viable flood alleviation scheme could be taken forward to prevent flooding to a small number of basements.  A second draft of the Dry Arch Feasibility Study, which has been received by my Department, has not yet been finalised as officials wish to thoroughly explore all options. It is anticipated at this stage that the study will be complete by 31 July 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the future of the St. Lucia site in Omagh.
answer_text: The St Lucia site remains in joint ownership between my Department and the Ministry of Defence (MOD) who retain ownership of the Historic Core.  My officials continue discussions with MOD colleagues regarding the transfer of this remaining part of the site.  You will be aware that the purpose of the gifting of this site was to generate revenue for the NI Executive.  However the transfer of the Historic Core in its current condition and given the current market conditions is likely to place a considerable financial burden on the Executive and be disproportionate to its market value.  Notwithstanding this, my officials continue to explore options with key stakeholders for the regeneration and future use of the St Lucia site so that it can be brought back into active use.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a timeline for the flood defence schemes planned for Ballycolman, Strabane.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether the contract for hedge cutting on rural roads has been awarded in West Tyrone for 2022.
answer_text: Responsibility for the maintenance of the vast majority of hedges that are next to the public road rests with the respective adjacent landowners.Any hedge cutting that my Department is responsible for is undertaken by our existing environmental contractors or by our in-house contractor.Both the Department, and private landowners, are bound by The Wildlife (Northern Ireland) Order 1985, which makes it a criminal offence to disturb or destroy nests and eggs. For this reason cutting hedges or trees from the start of March until the end of August is generally prohibited.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact the Section Office which covers the relevant section of West Tyrone: FermanaghandOmaghEast@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk or LondonderryandStrabane@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department are planning any road safety improvement schemes in Victoria Bridge, Tyrone.
answer_text: All four main routes into the centre of Victoria Bridge village have traffic calming measures in the form of road humps. My officials advise that a condition assessment has been completed which indicates some of these humps require maintenance work to reshape them. Similar remedial work has already been completed on the Melmount Road and work on the remaining approaches will be carried out as soon as resources become available.  My Department is also liaising with the PSNI and Derry City and Strabane District Council Policing & Community Safety Partnership to determine if additional road safety measures are required in Victoria Bridge.I am pleased to be able to confirm that the 2022/23 grass cutting programme has commenced and the grass verge on the A5 at Fyfin Road has been cut to improve visibility at the junction sightline.  My Department is also aware that vehicles park on the hard standing on the flyover at Victoria Bridge which can impair visibility for drivers of vehicles emerging from the slip road. Although no parking signs have been in place for a number or years in an effort to deter parking on the bridge, regrettably the problem persists and further interventions are being considered to seek to discourage parking at this location to improve road safety.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the resurfacing of the Dublin Road, Strabane.
answer_text: My officials advise that although the Dublin Road Industrial Estate is not currently adopted by my Department, an agreement in principle for the road to be brought up to an adoptable standard has been obtained.Following the satisfactory resolution of legal issues associated with the adoption process, it is my Department’s intention to resurface the Dublin Road Industrial Estate later this financial year, subject to the availability of contractor resource following the successful award of the new interim measured term surfacing contract.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department is in ownership of the former Castlederg railway building.
answer_text: I can confirm that my Department does not own the former Castlederg railway building, which Land Registry records indicate is privately owned.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on Youth Services in Castlederg.
answer_text: Centre Based Youth Services resumed in the Castlederg area with the reopening of the Derg Valley Youth Club on 8 March 2022 offering both centre-based generic non-targeted and targeted provision to the 61 junior members (9–12 years) and 22 senior members (13-17 years) registered with the club.Derg Valley Youth Club will continue working with primary and post primary schools in the area, including delivering the Transitions Programme to 3 primary schools and exploring opportunities during the 2022/23 academic year for collaboration with Castlederg High School. As well as a T:BUC Camp, a generic summer programme is planned focusing on Health and Wellbeing. The EA continues to offer a range of targeted services to children and young people in the Derg DEA area such as FLARE (Facilitating Life And Resilience Education), CADi, (Creative Arts and Digital Innovators ), OLS (Outdoor Learning Service), Horizons (Youth Leadership Programme) and the Youth Voice engagement initiative.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether schools have received budget allocations for the Engage II Programme, for the current financial year.
answer_text: The Engage programme has been extremely successful to date, with more than £24 million provided to 1,700 educational settings this academic year. Schools were notified on 28 March 2022 of their additional Engage II Programme funding to 30 June 2022.  This represents an additional £7.6m on top of the £16.6m already provided for the September 2021 to March 2022 period.Engage has ensured that our children and young people have been provided with a range of support to reduce the impact which the Covid-19 pandemic has had on their educational experience and learning. As regards the 2022/23 academic year, I issued a Direction to my officials for the Engage programme to continue to December 2022.  The Department of Finance approved that Direction on 6 July 2022, and I am delighted that I have been able to allocate £10million to continue the programme until the end of December 2022.   I have recently written to schools and settings to inform them.It is my intention that the programme will continue until the end of the academic year. However, as this remains subject to the necessary funding being made available, settings are not able to commit to expenditure beyond December 2022.  I will update schools on the budget position for the rest of the academic year as soon as I am in a position to do so.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the Engage II Programme funding for the current financial year.
answer_text: The Engage programme has been extremely successful to date, with more than £24 million provided to 1,700 educational settings this academic year. Schools were notified on 28 March 2022 of their additional Engage II Programme funding to 30 June 2022.  This represents an additional £7.6m on top of the £16.6m already provided for the September 2021 to March 2022 period.Engage has ensured that our children and young people have been provided with a range of support to reduce the impact which the Covid-19 pandemic has had on their educational experience and learning. I have recently written to schools and settings to inform them.It is my intention that the programme will continue until the end of the academic year. However, as this remains subject to the necessary funding being made available, settings are not able to commit to expenditure beyond December 2022.
